name: Home
title: Home
description: Homepage for the voiceover and acting portfolio, providing entry points to demos, showreels, planning tools, and newsletter signup.

core_features: Load homepage overview content (titles, key strengths, primary call-to-actions) using getHomePageContent and expose it to components that render summary text and navigation buttons.

Load a limited list of featured voiceover demos using getFeaturedVoiceoverDemos, support inline audio playback, show basic metadata (category, style, duration, ratings), and provide navigation to full demo details and the main voiceover page.

Load a limited list of featured showreels using getFeaturedShowreels, support inline video playback, show basic metadata (genre, year, duration), and provide navigation to full showreel details and the main acting page.

Load newsletter preference options using getNewsletterPreferencesOptions to dynamically build the footer signup form (interests, summary frequency options, weekly tips default, casting availability opt-in label).
Handle newsletter subscription submissions by validating user input, calling subscribeToNewsletter, and reflecting success or error messages without using localStorage.

Provide navigation to other major pages (Voiceover, Acting, Rates & Quotes, Project Planner, Clients & Testimonials, Contact / Booking) based on primary_call_to_actions.target_page from getHomePageContent and the specified outgoing URL parameter logic.
user_workflows: Browse featured voiceover demos: 1) User opens the Home page; 2) home-data-loader component invokes getFeaturedVoiceoverDemos; 3) featured-voiceover-demos-list receives the array of demos and renders one card per item; 4) User clicks a demo play control to start inline playback (component updates its internal playing state for that demo only); 5) User clicks the demo title or a detail action on a card, which triggers navigation to voiceover_demo_detail.html with URL parameter id set to the clicked demo's id; 6) User can click a link below the list that navigates to voiceover.html with URL parameter tab set to 'demos' so the Voiceover page opens in the correct tab.

Browse featured showreels: 1) User opens the Home page; 2) home-data-loader invokes getFeaturedShowreels; 3) featured-showreels-list receives the array and renders one card per showreel (using thumbnail and duration from the data); 4) User clicks a play control or thumbnail to start inline video playback for that showreel; 5) User clicks the card title or a detail action to navigate to showreel_detail.html with URL parameter id set to the selected showreel's id; 6) User can click a link below the list that navigates to acting.html with URL parameter tab set to 'showreels'.

Use primary navigation call-to-actions from Home: 1) home-data-loader invokes getHomePageContent; 2) primary-actions-panel receives hero_title, hero_subtitle, key_strengths, and primary_call_to_actions; 3) Component maps each primary_call_to_actions item to a button or link; 4) When the user clicks a call-to-action, the component inspects the target_page field and performs navigation: 'voiceover' -> voiceover.html?tab=demos, 'acting' -> acting.html?tab=showreels, 'rates_quotes' -> rates.html, 'project_planner' -> project_planner.html, 'clients_testimonials' -> clients_testimonials.html, 'contact_booking' -> contact_booking.html?tab=booking.

Sign up to the newsletter from the footer: 1) home-data-loader invokes getNewsletterPreferencesOptions; 2) newsletter-signup-form uses interest_options, summary_frequency_options, weekly_tips_default_enabled, and casting_availability_opt_in_label to build the form; 3) User enters an email address, selects one or more interests, chooses a summary frequency option, optionally toggles weekly tips and casting availability updates; 4) On form submit, the component validates that email is present and a summary_frequency option has been chosen; 5) If valid, it calls subscribeToNewsletter with email, the list of selected interest IDs, the selected summary_frequency ID, and boolean values for wants_weekly_tips and wants_casting_availability_updates; 6) On success, the component displays a confirmation message using the returned message and subscription fields; on failure (success is false), it displays the returned message as an error; 7) No subscription state is stored in localStorage; any visible confirmation state is maintained in the in-memory component state for the current page view.

Navigate to planning and reference tools from Home: 1) After getHomePageContent resolves, primary-actions-panel and/or secondary navigation components identify items in primary_call_to_actions with target_page values corresponding to rates, project planner, clients & testimonials, and contact/booking; 2) User clicks the relevant call-to-action; 3) The component performs navigation to the correct page with any required URL parameters: rates.html (no params), project_planner.html (no params), clients_testimonials.html (no params), contact_booking.html?tab=booking.
interactions: Click interaction on call-to-action items: Buttons or links created from getHomePageContent.primary_call_to_actions listen for onClick events, inspect the target_page field, and route to the corresponding URL, adding URL parameters where required (e.g., tab=demos for Voiceover, tab=showreels for Acting, tab=booking for Contact / Booking).

Click interaction on featured voiceover demo cards: Each card has an onClick handler on the play/pause control that toggles inline audio playback using the demo's audio_url, and another onClick handler on the title or detail control that navigates to voiceover_demo_detail.html with URL parameter id set to the demo's id.

Click interaction on the 'browse all voiceover demos' link: A link below or near the featured demos triggers navigation to voiceover.html with URL parameter tab set to 'demos', so the Voiceover page can read the tab parameter and default to the demos view.

Click interaction on featured showreel cards: Each card has an onClick handler on the play/thumbnail control that toggles inline video playback using the showreel's video_url, and another onClick handler on the title or detail control that navigates to showreel_detail.html with URL parameter id set to the showreel's id.

Click interaction on the 'browse all showreels' link: A link below or near the featured showreels triggers navigation to acting.html with URL parameter tab set to 'showreels', enabling the Acting page to show the showreels tab by default.

Form interactions within newsletter-signup-form: onChange handlers update the local form state whenever the user edits the email field, toggles interest checkboxes, selects a summary_frequency option, or toggles weekly tips / casting availability checkboxes; onSubmit validates the form and, if valid, calls subscribeToNewsletter; the component then reacts to the returned success flag and message to show either a success notification or an error state.

Playback state interactions for audio and video: When the user starts playback on a specific demo or showreel, the corresponding component sets that item's state to 'playing' and, optionally, stops playback on any previously playing item to prevent overlapping audio or video; this is handled in local component state without persisting to URL or localStorage.

Error-handling interactions for interface failures: If getHomePageContent, getFeaturedVoiceoverDemos, getFeaturedShowreels, or getNewsletterPreferencesOptions fail, their corresponding components show a generic, data-driven error or empty state message and may offer retry actions that re-trigger the same interface call on user click.
page_functionality.state_logic: The Home page does not require incoming URL parameters for its own internal state; it initializes entirely from the results of getHomePageContent, getFeaturedVoiceoverDemos, getFeaturedShowreels, and getNewsletterPreferencesOptions on each load. Component-level states (e.g., which demo is currently playing, which newsletter interests are selected, whether a subscription was just submitted) are maintained in memory within the page and are not persisted to localStorage. Cross-page state and navigation context are passed exclusively through URL parameters when the user leaves the Home page: (1) Navigating to voiceover.html always includes a tab URL parameter with value 'demos' so the Voiceover page can open the demos tab; (2) Navigating to acting.html always includes a tab URL parameter with value 'showreels' so the Acting page can open the showreels tab; (3) Navigating to contact_booking.html includes a tab URL parameter with value 'booking' so that page can default to its booking view; (4) Navigating to voiceover_demo_detail.html or showreel_detail.html includes an id URL parameter set to the selected item's id, so those pages can load the correct record. No state is stored in localStorage or other client-side persistence; reloading the Home page simply re-executes the assigned interfaces and reconstructs the view from their responses.

components.functionality: On initial page load, orchestrates all required data-fetching for the Home page by invoking getHomePageContent, getFeaturedVoiceoverDemos, getFeaturedShowreels, and getNewsletterPreferencesOptions, then exposes the resolved data (or errors) to child components via a shared state or props.

Receives hero_title, hero_subtitle, key_strengths, and primary_call_to_actions from the home-data-loader and renders a concise overview of the talent's offerings plus a set of action buttons that navigate to the main sections of the site based on primary_call_to_actions.target_page.

Displays the list of featured voiceover demos returned by getFeaturedVoiceoverDemos as interactive cards. Each card supports inline audio playback using audio_url, shows metadata such as category_label, style_label, display_duration, average_rating, review_count, and indicates whether the demo is already favorited using is_favorited. Cards provide navigation to the full demo detail page, and the component exposes a separate link to navigate to the full Voiceover page with the appropriate tab parameter.

Displays the list of featured showreels returned by getFeaturedShowreels as interactive cards. Each card supports inline video playback using video_url and thumbnail_url, shows metadata such as genre_label, year_filmed, and display_duration, and indicates whether the showreel is already part of a casting list using is_in_casting_list. Cards provide navigation to the showreel detail page, and the component exposes a separate link to navigate to the full Acting page with the appropriate tab parameter.

Uses primary_call_to_actions from getHomePageContent to render additional navigation links to Rates & Quotes, Project Planner, Clients & Testimonials, and Contact / Booking. It filters actions by target_page values related to these sections and wires each item to navigate to the corresponding page, adding any required URL parameters such as tab=booking for the Contact / Booking destination.

Builds and manages the newsletter signup form shown in the footer. It uses interest_options and summary_frequency_options from getNewsletterPreferencesOptions to construct dynamic checkbox and selection controls, uses weekly_tips_default_enabled to set the initial state of the weekly tips toggle, and uses casting_availability_opt_in_label for the casting availability checkbox label. On submit, it validates user input and calls subscribeToNewsletter with the chosen values, then reflects the result (success or error) in the UI using the returned success flag, message, and subscription object.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 2-column layout likely based on a centered max-width container (approx. 1080–1200px) with a simple percentage-based grid. Overall layout could easily map to a 12-column system (image ~5 columns, audio list ~7 columns).
layout.layout_pattern: The page keeps the mockup’s linear, top-to-bottom flow: a full-width black header bar with primary navigation (existing global element) sits at the very top. Below it, a generous vertical gap leads into a centered main content area. At the top of this main area is a large, centered page title. Under the title, the first major content band uses a two-column layout: a large, portrait-oriented hero image on the left and a stacked panel of primary actions and featured audio demos on the right. A second major content band follows beneath with another two-column layout: featured showreels on the left, and secondary navigation plus a newsletter signup on the right. All components sit within a common max-width centered container, echoing the mockup’s simple, 2-column grid feeling.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page keeps the mockup’s linear, top-to-bottom flow: a full-width black header bar with primary navigation (existing global element) sits at the very top. Below it, a generous vertical gap leads into a centered main content area. At the top of this main area is a large, centered page title. Under the title, the first major content band uses a two-column layout: a large, portrait-oriented hero image on the left and a stacked panel of primary actions and featured audio demos on the right. A second major content band follows beneath with another two-column layout: featured showreels on the left, and secondary navigation plus a newsletter signup on the right. All components sit within a common max-width centered container, echoing the mockup’s simple, 2-column grid feeling.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header occupies roughly 12–15% of the viewport height. The space from the bottom of the header to the bottom of the hero band is treated as the main ‘above the fold’ area: about 20–25% of that space is devoted to the title and its surrounding whitespace, and the remainder is shared between the left hero image and the right column (primary-actions-panel + featured-voiceover-demos-list). The second content band (showreels + navigation + newsletter) sits below with a clear section break, using similar horizontal width but slightly less vertical height than the hero band. Typical vertical gaps between major sections (header → title, title → hero band, hero band → showreels band) are in the 32–64px range, while intra-component padding remains in the 8–24px range to keep components airy.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout features a small number of focused elements with generous spacing, mirroring the mockup’s medium-low density. Each component gets clear breathing room around it: the featured lists have consistent vertical spacing between items, and the primary-actions-panel and newsletter-signup-form are padded cards or pill-like elements with comfortable internal spacing. There are no dense paragraphs; instead, key interactive items (CTAs, demos, showreels, navigation links, form fields) are spaced in a way that invites scanning without feeling crowded.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow extends the mockup’s Z-pattern. The eye starts at the top-left logo and travels along the black header bar to the right-side navigation. It then drops to the large, centered page title, then diagonally down and left to the colorful hero image, and across to the right column where the primary-actions-panel sits at the top. From there, the eye naturally descends through the stacked featured-voiceover-demos-list. As the user scrolls, the flow repeats in the second band: left-hand featured-showreels-list draws initial attention, then the eye moves across to the right-hand column with secondary-navigation-links and finally the newsletter-signup-form at the lower right, creating a layered yet simple scanning experience.

layout.overall_description: The homepage presents as a clean, centered stage framed by a bold black header at the top and generous white space below. The header stretches full width with the logo and main navigation, anchoring the experience. Below the header, the content sits inside a max-width container (roughly 1080–1200px) that’s centered on the page, with broad left and right margins so everything feels comfortably contained. A large, stylistic page title—set in script to echo the theatrical theme—sits alone near the top of this container, centered horizontally with generous space above and below. Directly beneath the title, the layout resolves into a two-column hero band: on the left, a large portrait-style image in a rounded rectangle provides the creative focal point; on the right, a vertically stacked set of pill-like CTAs and featured audio demos mirrors the mockup’s pill-style audio players. Scrolling down reveals a second two-column band aligned to the same grid: featured showreels as thumbnail cards on the left, and a column of secondary navigation links followed by a compact newsletter signup card on the right. Throughout, vertical spacing is consistent and generous, creating an easy, stage-like composition where each interactive element stands out clearly without feeling busy.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The home-data-loader overlays the main content area rather than occupying its own fixed slot in the layout. When active, it appears as a semi-opaque layer that starts directly below the black header bar and extends down to cover the entire centered content region, matching the max-width container’s horizontal boundaries. The overlay is horizontally centered with the same left and right margins as the main content, so it feels integrated with the layout rather than full-bleed. At the visual center of the hero band (roughly the midpoint between the hero image on the left and the audio/CTA column on the right), it displays a loading indicator or skeleton elements that align with the underlying two-column structure—e.g., a tall rounded rectangle where the hero image would be and several pill-shaped skeleton rows where the audio players and CTAs would appear. This keeps the loading state visually consistent with the finished layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a temporary absolute or fixed overlay within the main content area: anchored to the top edge just below the header and stretching to the bottom of the visible viewport, centered within the page’s max-width container. It sits above all other components in stacking order when active, then disappears entirely when data is loaded, allowing the normal layout to be fully interactive.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The primary-actions-panel is positioned in the top portion of the right column of the main hero band, directly to the right of the large hero image and aligned vertically so that its top edge sits slightly below the baseline of the centered page title. It spans the full width of the right column (roughly 55–60% of the central content width), leaving a consistent gutter of about 24–32px between it and the left hero image. The panel presents as a compact vertical stack of 2–3 pill-shaped action cards or large buttons (e.g., “Listen to Voiceover Demos,” “Watch Showreels,” “Plan Your Session”), each stretching almost the full width of this column. Each pill uses approximately 12–16px vertical padding and 20–24px horizontal padding, with 12–16px vertical gaps between them, maintaining the airy feel. The overall height of the primary-actions-panel occupies roughly the upper 25–30% of the right column, leaving clear space beneath it for the featured-voiceover-demos-list. A modest vertical gap (around 24px) separates the bottom of this panel from the first audio demo item, clearly distinguishing primary CTAs from the content list while keeping them visually connected.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the normal document flow, pinned to the top of the right column in the hero band. On narrower viewports, it should stack above the hero image and demos list, keeping its full-width pill style but preserving similar vertical spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The featured-voiceover-demos-list sits directly beneath the primary-actions-panel in the same right-hand hero column, forming a continuous vertical stack of pill-style audio players. It spans the full available width of that column and is aligned flush with the panel above, creating a clean right edge that mirrors the left edge of the hero image. Each demo item is a horizontally stretched pill or card that follows the visual language from the mockup: a rounded rectangle with a play button on the left, track title and brief meta text to the right, and generous internal padding (about 12–16px vertically and 20–24px horizontally). Each pill is separated by a vertical gap of around 16–20px. Immediately below each audio pill, a small descriptive label (e.g., “Commercial Reel,” “Narration Sample”) appears in a lighter, smaller type, left-aligned with the pill’s text and spaced 4–8px beneath the pill’s bottom edge. The list as a whole extends downward to roughly align with the bottom of the hero image in the left column, preserving a visually cohesive horizontal baseline at the bottom of the hero band. Generous white space is left below the last demo before the next major section begins, in the range of 32–48px.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right column of the hero band. It grows vertically as more demos are included, pushing the next section down while maintaining consistent spacing. On smaller screens, it stacks beneath the primary-actions-panel and may extend under the hero image once the layout collapses to a single column, but preserves its pill-stack appearance and vertical rhythm.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The featured-showreels-list occupies the left column of the second main content band, directly below the hero section. A generous vertical gap (about 48–64px) separates the bottom of the hero band from the top of this section, signaling a new content block while keeping continuity in the centered max-width container. Within this band, the left column aligns exactly beneath the hero image above, using approximately 45–50% of the container width. At the top of this column, a concise heading (e.g., “Showreels”) is left-aligned, with 16–24px of space above the first showreel card. Each showreel in the list appears as a card or thumbnail element that spans the full width of the column: a rectangular thumbnail (16:9 or similar), optionally with a play icon overlay, and text (title, short descriptor) below it. Cards use comfortable internal padding (12–16px around the text) and a moderate vertical gap of 24px between each. The stack of showreel cards occupies most of the vertical space in this second band’s left column, visually balancing against the combined height of the secondary-navigation-links and newsletter-signup-form on the right. The bottom of the final showreel card roughly lines up with or slightly exceeds the bottom of the newsletter card, giving the section a grounded horizontal closure.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the second band’s left column, flowing naturally as more showreels are added. On narrower screens, it stacks above the secondary-navigation-links and newsletter-signup-form, but retains its full-width card style and vertical spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The secondary-navigation-links group is positioned in the right column of the second main content band, aligned to mirror the width and horizontal placement of the hero band’s right column. It sits near the top of this column, with its top edge roughly level with the “Showreels” heading on the left. The group occupies roughly the upper 35–40% of the right column’s height. Links are presented as a small vertical stack of text-based navigation items or subtle pill-like buttons (e.g., “Pricing & Booking,” “Studio Specs,” “Full Portfolio”), each left-aligned, spanning about 80–100% of the column’s width. Each link has comfortable padding (8–12px vertically, 16–20px horizontally for pill-style; or sufficient line height and 8–12px vertical spacing for plain text links) with a vertical gap of around 12–16px between items. The block as a whole is separated from the newsletter-signup-form beneath it by a clear vertical gap of about 24–32px, visually grouping the navigation items together while acknowledging them as distinct from the newsletter card.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right column of the second content band. It retains its position at the top of this column at desktop widths. On smaller screens, it stacks above the newsletter-signup-form in a single column beneath the featured-showreels-list, preserving its vertical list structure and spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The newsletter-signup-form is placed directly below the secondary-navigation-links within the right column of the second content band, sharing the same horizontal alignment and width. It appears as a compact, self-contained card with a subtle border or shadow and softly rounded corners, spanning the full width of this column (roughly 40–45% of the central container). There is a distinct vertical separation from the links above (about 24–32px). Inside the card, padding of approximately 24px on all sides ensures a spacious feel: a short heading and optional one-line description sit at the top, followed by a single-line email input field that stretches nearly the full width of the card, and a submit button either right-aligned on the same row or placed as a full-width button directly beneath the input. Vertical spacing between these internal elements follows the 8–16px rhythm. The card’s height is moderate—roughly equivalent to the height of one showreel card plus a bit of breathing room—so that, in combination with the secondary-navigation-links above, the right column visually balances the taller stack of showreels on the left. A final section margin of about 48px is left below the newsletter card before any footer content (outside the scope of these components).
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right column of the second band. On smaller viewports, it appears below the secondary-navigation-links in the single-column flow, maintaining the same padded card appearance and vertical spacing relative to the content above and below.

name: Voiceover
title: Voiceover
description: Page for browsing, filtering, playing, favoriting, and shortlisting voiceover demos, with access to overview and studio/FAQ content.

core_features: Tab-based layout switching between Overview, Demos, and Studio / FAQs content using data from getVoiceoverPageContent, with the active tab determined by the URL parameter 'tab' or the API-provided default_tab.

Dynamic demo filtering in the Demos tab using categories, languages, styles, rating thresholds, duration ranges, and sort options provided by getVoiceoverFilterOptions, applied through searchVoiceoverDemos.
Inline playback of voiceover demos directly in the results list, using the audio_url from searchVoiceoverDemos, ensuring only one demo plays at a time.
Ability to mark demos as Favorites from the Demos list and to view current Favorites using addVoiceoverDemoToFavorites and getFavoriteVoiceoverDemos.
Ability to add demos to a Project Shortlist from the Demos list and to view the current Project Shortlist using addDemoToProjectShortlist and getProjectShortlist.

Navigation from demo titles in the Demos list to the Voiceover Demo Detail page (voiceover_demo_detail.html?id=...), and from actions in Overview/Demos to project_planner.html and rates.html with appropriate URL parameters.

URL-driven state for tab selection, filters, sorting, and pagination using the incoming parameters (tab, category, languages, style, min_duration_seconds, max_duration_seconds, min_average_rating, sort_by, page), with all changes reflected back into the query string instead of localStorage.
user_workflows: Workflow 1 – Arrive on Voiceover via main navigation and browse demos: 1) User opens voiceover.html via header/footer navigation without query parameters. 2) vo-page-init component calls getVoiceoverPageContent to obtain overview_html, studio_faq_html, and default_tab. 3) vo-page-init determines the active tab: if URL 'tab' is present, use it; otherwise use default_tab, which is expected to be configured so that navigation may default to 'demos'. 4) vo-page-init calls getVoiceoverFilterOptions to populate filter controls. 5) If the active tab is Demos, vo-page-init derives initial filters from URL parameters (if any) and calls searchVoiceoverDemos with these values plus page and page_size from the component state (page_size taken from the API response or configuration, not hardcoded). 6) In parallel, vo-page-init calls getFavoriteVoiceoverDemos and getProjectShortlist so the Demos list and summary panels can mark items as favorited or in the shortlist. 7) User sees the Demos tab selected, with filter controls, sort control, and a list of demos returned by searchVoiceoverDemos. 8) Clicking play on any demo in vo-demos-results-list starts audio playback via the demo’s audio_url and pauses any other currently playing demo. 9) User can navigate across pages using vo-demos-pagination, which updates the 'page' URL parameter and triggers new searchVoiceoverDemos calls while keeping the current filters and sort settings.

Workflow 2 – Use deep link with pre-applied category/style/rating filters (e.g., focusing on character demos): 1) User arrives on voiceover.html with query parameters such as tab=demos, category=character, style=video_game_animation, min_average_rating, max_duration_seconds, languages, and sort_by (values consistent with the enums and options returned by getVoiceoverFilterOptions and the VoiceoverDemo schema). 2) vo-page-init reads and stores all URL parameters and calls getVoiceoverPageContent and getVoiceoverFilterOptions. 3) Since tab=demos, vo-page-init calls searchVoiceoverDemos with category, languages (split from the comma-separated URL value), style, min_duration_seconds, max_duration_seconds, min_average_rating, sort_by, and page (defaulted if missing). 4) vo-demos-filter-bar pre-selects the relevant options (for example, the category option whose id matches 'character', the style option whose id matches the style parameter, and the language options that match the 'languages' parameter). 5) vo-demos-sort-control selects the sort option whose id matches sort_by (for example a descending rating sort if that option exists in sort_options). 6) The user reviews the filtered list, may adjust filters (e.g. tightening the rating threshold or duration range), which updates URL parameters and triggers new searchVoiceoverDemos calls with page reset to the first page. 7) The user can click any demo title to go to voiceover_demo_detail.html?id=<demoId> and later come back via links from the detail page that restore filters and pagination via URL parameters.

Workflow 3 – Mark demos as Favorites and review them: 1) While viewing the Demos tab, vo-demos-results-list displays each demo with an indicator for whether it is currently favorited (using the is_favorited field from searchVoiceoverDemos, synchronized with getFavoriteVoiceoverDemos). 2) When the user clicks the heart icon on a demo that is not favorited, vo-demos-results-list calls addVoiceoverDemoToFavorites with that demo’s id. 3) On success, the component updates the visual state of that demo to show it as favorited using the is_favorited flag returned in the response (if provided) or by re-deriving from context, and may optionally refresh the favorites summary by invoking getFavoriteVoiceoverDemos again. 4) vo-favorites-summary-panel shows the updated count and a list of favorite demos using getFavoriteVoiceoverDemos, allowing playback and navigation to the corresponding voiceover_demo_detail.html pages directly from that panel.

Workflow 4 – Build a Project Shortlist and proceed to planning: 1) While viewing the Demos tab, vo-page-init has already called getProjectShortlist to retrieve any existing shortlist and its items. 2) In vo-demos-results-list, each demo has a control (e.g., a button) to add it to the Project Shortlist if it is not already included (based on is_in_project_shortlist from searchVoiceoverDemos and the shortlist data from getProjectShortlist). 3) When the user clicks this control, vo-demos-results-list calls addDemoToProjectShortlist with the demo id. 4) On success, the component updates that demo’s status to show it is in the shortlist, using the shortlist object from the response, and passes the new shortlist to vo-project-shortlist-summary for display. 5) vo-project-shortlist-summary shows the updated total_items and list of items; from this panel the user can click a Plan project button that navigates to project_planner.html?title=VO%20Project%20Plan. 6) Within the shortlist summary, clicking an item’s title navigates to voiceover_demo_detail.html?id=<demoId> for more detailed information about the demo.

Workflow 5 – Navigate between tabs and other pages while preserving state: 1) The user may switch between Overview, Demos, and Studio / FAQs using vo-tab-switcher. 2) When switching to Overview or Studio / FAQs, vo-tab-switcher updates the 'tab' URL parameter and shows the corresponding content using overview_html or studio_faq_html from getVoiceoverPageContent without altering the current filter-related parameters. 3) When the user returns to the Demos tab, vo-page-init (or equivalent tab change handling) reuses the filter, sort, and pagination parameters currently in the URL to call searchVoiceoverDemos so that the user returns to the same results state. 4) From either Overview or Demos, clicking the Plan a project with these voiceover services button triggers navigation to project_planner.html with the title URL parameter set according to the defined outgoing connection. 5) From any commercial-category demo entry in vo-demos-results-list (where category matches the appropriate enum value for commercial work), the user can click a Get an instant quote link that navigates to rates.html?project_type=online_ad_web_commercial, allowing an instant quote to be computed on the Rates page based on that project type.

Workflow 6 – Return from Voiceover Demo Detail and restore list state: 1) The user navigates from the Demos list to a specific demo’s detail view via voiceover_demo_detail.html?id=<demoId>. 2) The detail page, when offering a Back to demos or View all voiceover demos link, includes URL parameters such as tab=demos, category, languages, style, min_duration_seconds, max_duration_seconds, min_average_rating, sort_by, and page that represent the state of the list at the time of navigation. 3) When the user follows that link back to voiceover.html, vo-page-init reads the parameters and sets its internal tab, filter, sort, and pagination state accordingly. 4) vo-page-init then calls searchVoiceoverDemos with those values so the Demos list is reconstructed to the same page and ordering, with the same filters applied, ensuring continuity for the user.
interactions: Tab switching: Clicking a tab label in vo-tab-switcher updates the active tab state, sets the 'tab' URL parameter, and conditionally renders vo-overview-section, vo-demos-section (filter bar, sort control, results list, pagination, favorites and shortlist panels), or vo-studio-faq-section.

Filter selection: Changing any filter control in vo-demos-filter-bar (category, language, style, rating threshold, duration range, or tags) updates the corresponding filter state, rewrites the matching URL parameters, resets the page to the first page, and triggers searchVoiceoverDemos with all current filters and sort_by so that the results list updates immediately.

Sort selection: Changing the sort option in vo-demos-sort-control updates the sort_by state and 'sort_by' URL parameter, resets the page to the first page, and triggers searchVoiceoverDemos with the new sort option applied.

Pagination: Clicking a page number or next/previous controls in vo-demos-pagination updates the 'page' URL parameter and calls searchVoiceoverDemos with the existing filters and sort, requesting the new page. The returned page and page_size values from searchVoiceoverDemos drive the pagination UI on subsequent renders.

Demo playback: Clicking the play/pause control for a demo in vo-demos-results-list starts or pauses playback using the demo’s audio_url. When a demo starts playing, the component pauses any other playing demo to prevent overlapping audio. Playback state is maintained in component state and is not encoded in the URL.

Favorite toggling (add): Clicking the heart icon for a non-favorited demo in vo-demos-results-list calls addVoiceoverDemoToFavorites with that demo’s id. On a successful response (success=true), the component updates the demo’s is_favorited state and, if needed, re-fetches the list of favorites using getFavoriteVoiceoverDemos so vo-favorites-summary-panel displays the current favorites. If is_favorited is already true, the control can be rendered as non-interactive or can ignore additional clicks, in line with the add-only behavior of the interface.

Shortlist add: Clicking the Add to shortlist control for a demo not yet in the shortlist in vo-demos-results-list calls addDemoToProjectShortlist with that demo’s id. On a successful response, the returned shortlist object is used to update both the per-demo is_in_project_shortlist state and the vo-project-shortlist-summary component. Demos already in the shortlist can render this control as active/disabled and ignore further clicks.

Demo detail navigation: Clicking a demo’s title in vo-demos-results-list or vo-favorites-summary-panel navigates to voiceover_demo_detail.html with the query parameter id set to that demo’s id, following the outgoing connection definition. Additional list state restoration is handled through the detail page’s own links back to voiceover.html with the appropriate parameters.

Project planner navigation: Clicking the Plan a project with these voiceover services button in vo-overview-section or vo-project-shortlist-summary navigates to project_planner.html?title=VO%20Project%20Plan using standard browser navigation.

Rates navigation: For demos whose category indicates commercial work, clicking the Get an instant quote link in vo-demos-results-list navigates to rates.html?project_type=online_ad_web_commercial using standard browser navigation, enabling a pre-selected project type on the Rates page.

URL/back-forward handling: When the user uses the browser back/forward buttons, the vo-url-state-sync component listens to history events (e.g., popstate), re-parses the URL parameters, updates internal state (tab, filters, sort_by, page), and triggers searchVoiceoverDemos (for the Demos tab) so that the UI reflects the state encoded in the URL instead of relying on localStorage.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page uses URL parameters as the single source of truth for tab selection and list state, with no reliance on localStorage. On initial load, vo-page-init parses the query string for: tab (string), category (string), languages (string, comma-separated), style (string), min_duration_seconds (number), max_duration_seconds (number), min_average_rating (number), sort_by (string), and page (number). If 'tab' is missing, vo-page-init uses default_tab from getVoiceoverPageContent as the active tab. If page is missing or invalid, a default page index (e.g., the first page) is assumed in internal state, but subsequent navigation always writes the explicit 'page' parameter. For language filters, the 'languages' URL parameter (if present) is split on commas and matched against the languages list returned by getVoiceoverFilterOptions; any values that do not match available options are ignored to avoid invalid states. For filters such as category, style, and rating threshold, vo-demos-filter-bar uses the ids returned by getVoiceoverFilterOptions and attempts to pre-select the option whose id matches the corresponding URL parameter; if no match is found, that filter falls back to an unselected or default option derived from the API response (for example, an option designated by ordering or configuration rather than being hardcoded). The duration range controls are initialized using duration_range from getVoiceoverFilterOptions and, when URL parameters for min_duration_seconds and/or max_duration_seconds are present, those values are clamped to the allowed range before being used as control values and as arguments to searchVoiceoverDemos. Whenever the user changes a filter, sort option, tab, or page, the relevant parameters are updated in the URL (e.g., via history.pushState or history.replaceState) by vo-url-state-sync so that the current state is always reproducible via the URL alone. The searchVoiceoverDemos call is always driven by the latest in-memory state that mirrors the URL parameters, ensuring that reloading the page or following a shared link reproduces the same Demos view. Favorites and project shortlist membership are not encoded in the URL because they are user-specific and handled by getFavoriteVoiceoverDemos, addVoiceoverDemoToFavorites, getProjectShortlist, and addDemoToProjectShortlist; however, the visual indicators for these states are recomputed after each relevant API call and after each searchVoiceoverDemos response using the is_favorited and is_in_project_shortlist flags and the data from the favorites and shortlist APIs.

components.functionality: Initializes the Voiceover page by parsing URL parameters, determining the active tab, loading static/semi-static content, loading filter metadata, and triggering initial demo search, favorites retrieval, and project shortlist retrieval as needed.
Keeps the browser URL query string synchronized with the internal page state for tab, filters, sort, and page, and listens for browser navigation events to restore state from the URL.
Displays and manages the three primary tabs (Overview, Demos, Studio / FAQs), switches visible content sections, and updates the 'tab' URL parameter.
Renders the overview_html provided by getVoiceoverPageContent when the Overview tab is active and exposes a button to navigate to the project planner with voiceover-focused context.
Renders the studio_faq_html provided by getVoiceoverPageContent when the Studio / FAQs tab is active and can optionally expose the same project planning navigation as the Overview tab.

Container for all Demos-tab-specific components, including filter bar, sort control, results list, pagination, favorites summary, and shortlist summary. It coordinates calls to searchVoiceoverDemos based on changes in filters, sorting, or pagination.

Provides controls for selecting category, language(s), style, rating thresholds, duration range, and tags for the Demos list, using options from getVoiceoverFilterOptions and current values from URL parameters. On any change, it updates the URL and requests a refreshed demo list.
Displays available sort options for demos using the sort_options returned by getVoiceoverFilterOptions and allows the user to change sort order, updating the sort_by state and URL parameter.

Displays a paginated list of demos returned by searchVoiceoverDemos, including title, category_label, language, style_label, display_duration, description_snippet, rating_label, reviews badges, and inline audio playback controls. Provides controls for marking demos as favorites, adding demos to the project shortlist, navigating to demo detail pages, and navigating to the Rates page for relevant demos.
Renders pagination controls based on total_results, page, and page_size from searchVoiceoverDemos, and updates the 'page' URL parameter plus triggers new search requests when the user changes pages.

Shows a concise list or count of currently favorited demos and optionally a subset of favorite items, retrieved via getFavoriteVoiceoverDemos. Allows playback and navigation to individual demo detail pages from the favorites list.

Displays information about the current Project Shortlist using getProjectShortlist or the shortlist returned by addDemoToProjectShortlist, including shortlist name, total_items, and a list of items with quick links to demo detail pages and a button to navigate to the Project Planner.

Monitors the output of searchVoiceoverDemos and, when total_results is zero for the current filters, displays an informational message and a control allowing the user to clear or relax filters via vo-demos-filter-bar.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 2-column layout likely based on a centered max-width container (approx. 1080–1200px) with a simple percentage-based grid. Overall layout could easily map to a 12-column system (image ~5 columns, audio list ~7 columns).
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom flow: a full-width fixed site header (outside this spec) → generous vertical gap → centered page title 'Voiceover' in the same theatrical script style as the 'Acting' heading → another vertical gap → a centered tab switcher. Below the tabs, the active tab’s content appears inside a max-width, centered content band (~1080–1200px). For the Demos tab, the content band uses a two-column layout mirroring the mockup: a left column for supporting/summarizing content, and a right column for the stacked audio demo players and their controls.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom flow: a full-width fixed site header (outside this spec) → generous vertical gap → centered page title 'Voiceover' in the same theatrical script style as the 'Acting' heading → another vertical gap → a centered tab switcher. Below the tabs, the active tab’s content appears inside a max-width, centered content band (~1080–1200px). For the Demos tab, the content band uses a two-column layout mirroring the mockup: a left column for supporting/summarizing content, and a right column for the stacked audio demo players and their controls.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Roughly 12–15% of viewport height is reserved for the global black header. The remaining vertical space begins with ~20–25% for the 'Voiceover' title and tab switcher area (hero region with generous top and bottom padding), and the remainder for the tab content. Within the Demos tab, the two-column area allocates about 35–40% of the content width to the left column (summary panels) and 60–65% to the right column (filters, list, pagination). Horizontally, the main content is centered with combined left/right margins of about 15–20% of viewport width. Vertical gaps between major blocks (header → title, title → tabs, tabs → content sections, between major subsections) follow the 32–64px spacing range.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout is moderately spacious. Major blocks (title, tabs, each tab section, two-column demos area) have clear breathing room with 32–48px gaps. Interactive controls such as filter chips, tab labels, and audio player rows use 8–16px vertical and 16–24px horizontal padding to maintain the airy feel. The demos list maintains a clear vertical rhythm, with consistent spacing between players. Text-heavy areas (Overview, Studio/FAQ) use a single-column layout with generous line spacing and margins so nothing feels cramped.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow adopts the mockup’s Z-pattern. The eye moves across the fixed black header (logo → nav → icons), then drops to the centered 'Voiceover' heading. From there it moves down to the horizontally centered tab switcher, then into the active tab. In the Demos tab, the eye initially lands on the larger right column where the filters and first demo players sit (primary interaction zone), then optionally glances left to the summary panels. Within the right column, the user scans from filter bar and sort control at the top, down through the stacked players, and finally to the pagination at the bottom. This reinforces a top-to-bottom, right-leaning vertical scan path similar to the image-to-audio flow of the original mockup.

layout.overall_description: Beneath a full-width, fixed black site header, the Voiceover page content is centered in a wide, white canvas constrained by a max-width container. A generous vertical gap leads into a large, theatrical script heading 'Voiceover' set dead center, echoing the dramatic 'Acting' title from the mockup. Below the title, another band of whitespace introduces a slim, pill-style tab switcher, also centered, letting visitors flip between Overview, Studio & FAQ, and Demos without visual clutter. When Overview or Studio & FAQ are active, their content appears as a single, elegant column of text and supporting elements centered in the page, with ample padding around each block. When Demos is active, the layout shifts into a two-column composition reminiscent of the mockup: the left side is a compact, vertically stacked column of clean, card-like summary panels for favorites and project shortlist, while the right side becomes the main stage—a tall, vertically flowing stack of audio demos. At the top of this right column, a horizontal filter bar and right-aligned sort control sit like a control console; beneath them, wide, pill-shaped audio players march down the page with consistent spacing, each dominating the full width of the right column. Below the last demo, pagination controls tuck neatly into the lower right, giving the section a clear visual endpoint. Overall, the composition feels minimal yet theatrical—strong black and white, clear structure, generous breathing room, and a right-weighted vertical scan path that makes browsing and managing voiceover demos feel focused and effortless.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This is a non-visual controller that initializes the Voiceover page state as soon as the content area loads. It does not render any visible UI and therefore occupies no visual space in the layout; it sits conceptually behind the entire centered content band beneath the global header.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual; runs in the background and does not affect spatial layout directly.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This is an invisible state manager that synchronizes the current tab, filters, and pagination with the URL. It has no on-screen representation and does not alter spacing or positioning. It operates behind the scenes for the entire Voiceover content area.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual; no size or position in the rendered layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The tab switcher is centered horizontally beneath the large 'Voiceover' heading, sitting within the same max-width content container. It appears about 32–40px below the bottom of the heading, forming a slim horizontal strip of pill-like tab labels (e.g., 'Overview', 'Studio & FAQ', 'Demos'). The switcher spans roughly 60% of the content width and is aligned to the center, leaving equal white space on the left and right. Each tab label has 8–12px vertical and 16–24px horizontal padding, with ample 16–24px gaps between labels, preserving the airy, theatrical feel. The entire tab row has a low visual weight border or underline that echoes the minimal styling from the mockup, and acts as the visual anchor for the content sections below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element within the flow of the page, scrolling naturally with the content beneath the fixed global header.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Overview section occupies the main content area directly below the tab switcher when the 'Overview' tab is active. It appears approximately 32–48px beneath the tabs, centered within the max-width container. The section uses a single-column layout between 60–70% of the container width, centered so that generous left and right margins mirror the spacious margins of the mockup. Inside, text, images, or key points flow vertically with 24–32px gaps between paragraphs or subsections. Top and bottom padding inside the section follow the 24–32px range, giving the Overview a clean, editorial-card feel without heavy borders, and leaving sufficient white space before the next major section or the page bottom.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Shown or hidden based on the active tab; otherwise behaves as a normal block-level section that scrolls with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Studio & FAQ section occupies the same spatial slot as the Overview section, appearing directly below the tab switcher when the 'Studio & FAQ' tab is active. It also uses a centered single-column layout about 60–70% of the container width, with wide white margins on either side. The top of the section begins 32–48px below the tabs. Within the section, questions and answers are stacked vertically, each Q&A block separated by 24–32px vertical spacing following the page’s relaxed rhythm. Section-level padding is 24–32px on all sides to give FAQs room to breathe, mirroring the balance and white space of the mockup’s main content area.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Mutually exclusive with other tab sections; displays as a normal flowing block only when its tab is selected.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Demos section appears when the 'Demos' tab is active, starting about 32–48px below the tab switcher. It spans the full width of the central content container but is internally divided into a two-column layout mirroring the mockup’s image-and-audio structure. The left column occupies approximately 35–40% of the container width and runs from near the top of the section to near its bottom; this column hosts summary panels and remains visually lighter. The right column occupies roughly 60–65% of the width and is aligned so that its left edge is roughly at the visual center of the page, making it the primary column. There is a 32–40px horizontal gap between the columns, preserving clarity. Vertically, the right column begins at the same height as the top of the left column content, creating a single aligned shelf, and both columns extend downwards as needed with consistent 24–32px gaps between internal components. Overall, this section takes up the majority of the viewport height when populated, reinforcing its role as the main working area for voiceover demos.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows within the page under the tabs, adapting its column heights to content; on narrower viewports it can collapse to stacked columns, but in the primary design it remains a two-column layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The filter bar sits at the very top of the Demos section’s right column, acting as the entry point for interacting with the demo list. It aligns flush with the left edge of the right column and spans nearly its full width (minus small internal padding). The bar follows a pill-like, minimal style: horizontally arranged controls such as category chips or dropdowns, with 8–12px vertical and 16–24px horizontal padding around them. There is about 24px vertical space between the top of the right column and the filter controls, aligning vertically with the content in the left column. On wide screens, the filter bar creates a visual horizontal bar across the upper part of the right column, sitting slightly below and visually parallel to the top of the summary panels on the left.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right column, scrolling away with the rest of the demos content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The sort control is positioned in the right column in close relationship to the filter bar. On wide layouts, it sits on the same horizontal row as the filter bar, right-aligned within the right column so that the filter controls occupy the left portion and the sort dropdown aligns with the right edge, creating a balanced control strip. There is a small 8–16px horizontal gap between the end of the filter controls and the sort control, and they share the same vertical center line. If the space is constrained, the sort control shifts to a row immediately beneath the filter bar but remains right-aligned, with about 8–12px vertical spacing between them. The control itself is compact, with 8–12px vertical and 16px horizontal padding, matching the pill-like minimal style.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline with or directly beneath the filter bar inside the right column; scrolls with the demo list.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The results list occupies the bulk of the Demos section’s right column, starting approximately 24–32px below the filter/sort row. It spans the full width of the right column, from left to right padding, forming a vertical stack of pill-style audio demo cards. Each demo entry is a wide, horizontally oriented pill with rounded corners, aligned to the edges of the right column, and given 12–16px vertical and 16–24px horizontal internal padding. The demo title, play controls, and meta information are arranged within this pill, following the clean minimal aesthetic. Between each demo pill, there is a consistent 16–24px vertical gap, establishing a clear vertical rhythm similar to the stacked audio players in the mockup. The list expands downward as needed, and the vertical edge of this right column remains visually dominant relative to the lighter left column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Expands vertically based on the number of demos; scrolls normally within the page. It is replaced visually by the empty-state component when no results exist.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The pagination controls sit at the bottom of the Demos section’s right column, providing a clear visual endpoint to the list. They appear about 24–32px below the final demo card in the results list. The pagination row is horizontally aligned so that its right edge lines up with the right edge of the right column, with page numbers or next/prev buttons arranged in a compact row. The control row itself is relatively low-profile, occupying only enough height for a single line of buttons with 8–12px vertical and 12–16px horizontal padding per control. A small amount of white space (24–32px) separates the pagination from the bottom of the Demos section, echoing the breathing room of other section boundaries.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Anchored to the bottom of the results list area in the right column; scrolls with the page and only appears when there are enough demos to paginate.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The favorites summary panel is the top-most element in the Demos section’s left column. It aligns with the left edge of the left column and spans its full width, forming a vertically oriented card. The panel sits approximately 24–32px below the top of the Demos section, vertically aligned with the filter/sort strip in the right column to create a unified top baseline across both columns. It has a subtle background or border to differentiate it from the white page, with 16–24px internal padding on all sides. Inside, favorite demo counts, quick actions, or short lists stack vertically with 12–16px spacing. The panel’s overall height is moderate, occupying roughly the upper third to half of the left column’s vertical space, depending on content, ensuring it feels like a concise snapshot rather than a dominant block.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the left column, scrolling with the page; it does not overlay the right column and does not stick on scroll.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The project shortlist summary panel is positioned directly beneath the favorites summary panel in the left column. There is a 24–32px vertical gap between the bottom of the favorites panel and the top of this panel, matching the generous section spacing elsewhere. This panel also spans the entire width of the left column, with the same card-like treatment and 16–24px internal padding. It may be slightly taller than the favorites panel if it contains more detail, but overall it remains visually secondary to the demo list on the right. Together, the two panels create a vertically stacked, columnar block on the left that visually parallels the large image block in the original mockup, providing a stable left anchor while still allowing the right column to dominate attention.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block within the left column, scrolling normally beneath the favorites panel; its height is driven by its content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The empty state occupies the same spatial region as the results list within the right column when no demos match the current filters. It is centered within the right column, both horizontally and with comfortable vertical margins. The empty-state container begins approximately 24–32px below the filter/sort strip, just as the results list would, and extends downwards with enough height to feel substantial—roughly equivalent to a few stacked demo cards. Inside, an icon or illustration (optional) appears centered at the top of this area, with a concise message and suggested next actions stacked below, each separated by 8–16px. There is ample white space surrounding the content, emphasizing the minimal, calm aesthetic rather than clutter. The pagination control below is hidden when the empty state is shown, allowing the empty-state component to be the clear focal point in the right column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally replaces the results list in the right column when there are zero demos; otherwise it is not rendered and occupies no space.

name: Voiceover Demo Detail
title: Voiceover Demo Detail
description: Displays full details for a selected voiceover demo, including audio playback, ratings, contextual tags, favorites control, project shortlist actions, and navigation back to the filtered demos list.

core_features: Load and display a specific voiceover demo using getVoiceoverDemoDetail based on the id URL parameter.
Provide an audio player to play/pause the demo, showing its runtime using duration_seconds and display_duration.
Show contextual information for the demo (title, category, style, language, description, tags) to help users evaluate suitability.
Display an overall rating summary using average_rating, review_count, rating_label, and reviews_badge_text, with a compact list of recent_reviews.
Allow users to add the demo to their Favorites list via addVoiceoverDemoToFavorites, updating the UI based on is_favorited and the API result.
Allow users to add the demo to their current Project Shortlist via addDemoToProjectShortlist, then show updated shortlist context using getProjectShortlist.
Indicate whether the demo is already part of a project shortlist using is_in_project_shortlist and the current shortlist data.

Provide a breadcrumb/back link that returns the user to voiceover.html with their previous demos tab, filters, pagination, and sort state preserved using return_category, return_page, and return_sort_by.
Offer a call-to-action to create a project plan from this demo, navigating to project_planner.html with a prefilled title parameter as specified in the outgoing navigation configuration.
user_workflows: View a demo and its details: 1) User navigates to voiceover_demo_detail.html with an id URL parameter (and optionally return_category, return_page, return_sort_by). 2) On page load, the demo-detail loader component reads id and calls getVoiceoverDemoDetail(demoId). 3) The page binds the returned data to the audio player, metadata panel (title, category_label, style_label, language, tags, description, display_duration), rating summary, and recent_reviews list. 4) The page also calls getProjectShortlist to retrieve any existing shortlist and display a mini-panel and status for whether the current demo is already in the shortlist.

Add a demo to Favorites from the detail view: 1) After the demo data loads, the favorites control checks is_favorited. 2) If is_favorited is false, an actionable 'Add to Favorites' button is shown; if true, a non-clickable 'In Favorites' state is shown. 3) When the user clicks 'Add to Favorites', the component calls addVoiceoverDemoToFavorites with the current demo id. 4) On a successful response (success is true), the component updates local page state to set is_favorited to the returned is_favorited value and may display the favorites_total count in the UI. 5) The button switches to a non-clickable 'In Favorites' state to avoid duplicate calls.

Add a demo to the Project Shortlist: 1) After loading the demo with getVoiceoverDemoDetail, the page reads is_in_project_shortlist and also loads the current shortlist with getProjectShortlist. 2) If the demo is not in the shortlist, the 'Add to Project Shortlist' button is enabled; if it is already present, the button is disabled or shown as 'In Shortlist'. 3) When the button is clicked, the component calls addDemoToProjectShortlist with the current demo id. 4) On successful response, the component updates its shortlist state using the returned shortlist object (id, name, total_items, items). 5) The page updates the demo’s shortlist status to reflect that it is now in the shortlist (via is_in_project_shortlist in local state) and refreshes the mini shortlist panel.

Review shortlist context from the demo page: 1) When the page loads, getProjectShortlist is called without parameters. 2) If exists is false, the shortlist mini-panel indicates that no shortlist currently exists. 3) If exists is true, the panel displays shortlist.name (if any), total_items, and a truncated list of items. 4) When the current demo is part of items (matching demo_id), the 'Add to Project Shortlist' action is disabled or visually marked complete for this demo.

Return to the filtered demos list with state preserved: 1) When voiceover_demo_detail.html is opened from voiceover.html, the source page appends return_category, return_page, and return_sort_by URL parameters. 2) The breadcrumb component reads these parameters on load. 3) When the user clicks the 'Back to filtered demos' breadcrumb, the component constructs a URL to voiceover.html with tab=demos and passes category, page, and sort_by values from the corresponding return_* parameters when they are present. 4) The browser navigates to that URL, allowing voiceover.html to restore the previous filter, page, and sort order state for comparison.

Create a project plan from this demo: 1) Once the demo is displayed, the user may click a 'Create project plan from this demo' call-to-action button. 2) The button does not call any API; instead, it triggers navigation to project_planner.html with the title URL parameter set to the preconfigured encoded value. 3) The project_planner.html page uses this title parameter to initialize a new plan related to the chosen demo.
interactions: On page mount, the demo-detail loader reads the id URL parameter; if it is present, it calls getVoiceoverDemoDetail with that value and populates page state with the returned demo fields.
On page mount, independently of demo loading, the shortlist mini-panel calls getProjectShortlist to fetch any existing shortlist and stores exists and shortlist in its local state.

The audio player exposes play, pause, and seek controls; when the user clicks play, the component uses the audio_url to start playback and displays the current time versus display_duration, without hardcoding any lengths.

Clicking the 'Add to Favorites' button (when enabled) calls addVoiceoverDemoToFavorites with the current demo id; based on the response, the UI updates is_favorited and optionally shows the favorites_total count (for example, in a badge). Subsequent clicks are prevented once the demo is marked as favorited.

Clicking the 'Add to Project Shortlist' button (when enabled) calls addDemoToProjectShortlist with the current demo id; on success, the component merges the returned shortlist data into its state and disables the button for this demo.
If the shortlist mini-panel is visible, any update to the shortlist (from addDemoToProjectShortlist) immediately rebinds its list of items and total_items to reflect the latest shortlist content.
Clicking the breadcrumb/back link constructs a voiceover.html URL using tab=demos and any available return_category, return_page, and return_sort_by URL parameters, then navigates to that URL.
Clicking the 'Create project plan from this demo' CTA navigates to project_planner.html with a fixed title URL parameter, without persisting any other state from this page.

If getVoiceoverDemoDetail returns status flags like is_favorited or is_in_project_shortlist, the favorites and shortlist buttons initialize in corresponding disabled or completed states, and do not issue API calls until an enabled button is clicked.
page_functionality.state_logic: The main page state is derived from URL parameters and API responses only; no localStorage or other persistent browser storage is used. The required id parameter determines which demo is loaded: on initial render, the page reads id from the query string and passes it to getVoiceoverDemoDetail to populate the demo detail state (audio_url, title, category, style, language, display_duration, description, tags, ratings, recent_reviews, is_favorited, is_in_project_shortlist). The optional return_category, return_page, and return_sort_by parameters are not sent to APIs; instead, they are stored in in-memory state to construct the 'Back to filtered demos' URL for navigation. When the favorites button is used, the page updates an in-memory flag for is_favorited based only on the addVoiceoverDemoToFavorites response; it does not change the URL. Similarly, when addDemoToProjectShortlist is called, the returned shortlist object and the current demo’s shortlist membership are stored in memory and used to render the shortlist mini-panel and button state. The getProjectShortlist call at load time populates existing shortlist state if any; if the user navigates away and back, the shortlist state is reloaded fresh via this interface. No state is serialized to the URL beyond id and the return_* parameters used solely for navigation back to voiceover.html.

components.functionality: Initializes the page by reading the id URL parameter, calling getVoiceoverDemoDetail with that id, and storing the returned demo object in shared page state for other components to consume.

Displays a breadcrumb/back link that returns the user to voiceover.html, preserving demos filters, page number, and sort order using the optional return_category, return_page, and return_sort_by URL parameters.

Shows the main identifying information for the demo, including title, category_label, style_label, language, display_duration, and description, so users can quickly understand what type of voiceover the demo represents.
Displays the demo’s tags and category context to help users recognize usage types, styles, or other attributes associated with this demo.
Provides audio playback controls for the selected demo using its audio_url, showing current playback time and total duration based on display_duration and/or duration_seconds.

Shows the demo’s overall rating using average_rating and rating_label, together with review_count and reviews_badge_text (including any '10+ reviews' style badge) so users can quickly assess social proof.
Displays a concise list of recent_reviews for the demo, showing each review’s reviewer_name, rating, comment, and date to provide qualitative feedback for casting decisions.

Enables the user to add the demo to their Favorites list by calling addVoiceoverDemoToFavorites. The component checks is_favorited to determine whether to show an enabled 'Add to Favorites' button or a non-interactive 'In Favorites' indicator, and updates its state based on the API response.

Allows the user to add the current demo to their Project Shortlist by calling addDemoToProjectShortlist. It uses is_in_project_shortlist and the current shortlist data to determine whether the action is available, and disables the button when the demo is already in the shortlist.

Shows a compact view of the current Project Shortlist so the user can see how this demo fits into the larger selection. It displays shortlist.name (if available), total_items, and a brief list of items containing basic info like demo_title, category_label, display_duration, and added_at.

Provides a 'Create project plan from this demo' call-to-action that navigates the user to project_planner.html with a preconfigured title URL parameter, without calling additional APIs from this page.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 2-column layout likely based on a centered max-width container (approx. 1080–1200px) with a simple percentage-based grid. Overall layout could easily map to a 12-column system (image ~5 columns, audio list ~7 columns).
layout.layout_pattern: Below the existing full-width black header bar, all page content sits inside a centered, white main container with a max width of about 1080–1200px. The flow is linear top-to-bottom: a slim breadcrumb row at the very top of the content area → a generous, full-width demo header panel acting as the hero/title area → a compact horizontal tag strip directly under the header → a main two-column section where the left column focuses on ratings and reviews (the more text-based “context block”) and the right column contains the primary audio player and related controls (the main media/action block). Secondary controls like favorites and shortlist are grouped tightly around the audio player, while the shortlist mini-panel and project plan CTA are placed to the lower-right to support the primary listening flow without competing visually.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Below the existing full-width black header bar, all page content sits inside a centered, white main container with a max width of about 1080–1200px. The flow is linear top-to-bottom: a slim breadcrumb row at the very top of the content area → a generous, full-width demo header panel acting as the hero/title area → a compact horizontal tag strip directly under the header → a main two-column section where the left column focuses on ratings and reviews (the more text-based “context block”) and the right column contains the primary audio player and related controls (the main media/action block). Secondary controls like favorites and shortlist are grouped tightly around the audio player, while the shortlist mini-panel and project plan CTA are placed to the lower-right to support the primary listening flow without competing visually.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header bar occupies about 12–15% of viewport height and remains visually dominant but separate. The main content stack fills the rest: ~10% for the breadcrumb + top breathing room, ~20–25% for the demo header and its top/bottom spacing (mirroring the large ‘Acting’ title region), and the remaining ~60–65% for the two-column content. Horizontally, the main container is centered with combined left/right margins around 15–20% of the viewport width, leaving a central band for content. Within this band, the two columns roughly follow a 5/12 (left) to 7/12 (right) split, separated by a 32–40px gap. Components generally respect an 8px base spacing system: 32–48px between major sections, 16–24px between related components in the same section, and 8–16px internal padding within cards and controls.
layout_strategies.content_density: The density remains moderately spacious. Each major block—breadcrumb strip, demo header, tags row, and the two-column section—has clear vertical separation. The audio player and its associated controls are stacked with consistent spacing, and rating/review cards are allowed ample padding and margin, avoiding any sense of clutter. Text-heavy elements (reviews) are constrained in width (left column only) to maintain readability, while interactive elements (audio player, favorites, shortlist, CTA) are grouped but not crowded. There are no dense grids or tables; the layout emphasizes a few key interactions with room to breathe.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual path follows a Z-pattern similar to the mockup: the eye moves left-to-right across the fixed black header, then drops into the main content, catching the left-aligned breadcrumb first, then the centered demo title within the demo header. From there the eye moves down and naturally shifts to the right, landing on the prominent audio player at the top of the right column (primary interaction). Once the main media is noticed, the gaze sweeps left to the rating summary, then down to recent reviews, and back to the right for secondary actions (favorites, shortlist, plan CTA, and the shortlist mini-panel). The stacked vertical elements in each column encourage effortless top-to-bottom scanning, echoing the audio list stack from the original mockup.

layout.overall_description: Within a full-width black header and navigation at the very top, the Voiceover Demo Detail page sits on a clean white canvas, centered in the browser window with wide side margins. At the top of this white content area, a slim breadcrumb line runs along the left edge of the central content band, giving a subtle contextual anchor without dominating. Below it, a generous demo header panel acts as the visual hero: the demo title and key meta information sit in a horizontally centered block with plenty of whitespace above and below, echoing the theatrical ‘Acting’ title in the mockup. Immediately underneath, a horizontal row of compact pill-style tags spans the content width, left-aligned and loosely wrapping as needed, giving the feeling of a light, flexible metadata strip. After a comfortable vertical gap, the page resolves into a two-column layout: on the left, a tall, card-like stack for rating summary and recent reviews forms a stable, more text-based pillar; on the right, a wider column holds a large pill-shaped audio player at the top, followed closely by favorites and shortlist controls and a strong project plan CTA. A condensed shortlist mini-panel sits toward the lower-right of the main content, almost like a small, anchored toolbox, summarizing what’s been shortlisted. The relationship between these blocks keeps the design airy and modern: bold blacks and whites, soft rounded rectangles, and a measured grid of vertical stacks and horizontal pairings, all reinforcing a clear first action—listen to the demo—followed by opportunities to rate, review, favorite, and add to projects.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader appears as a semi-opaque overlay that covers the entire main content region below the fixed black header. It aligns with the centered content container horizontally, extending from just under the header down to the bottom of the viewport. In the middle of this overlay, a subtle loading indicator (spinner or progress element) is centered both vertically and horizontally within the content band, ensuring it sits above the area where the demo header and two-column layout will appear. The overlay slightly dims the underlying white background while still hinting at the layout beneath, reinforcing the sense of a single-page, full-screen loading state without obscuring the global header.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed over the main content area when active, positioned relative to the viewport (excluding the header) so it stays centered while scrolling is disabled or discouraged.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The breadcrumb navigation sits at the very top of the main white content area, directly beneath the full-width black header. It is left-aligned within the central content container, starting at roughly the same horizontal position as the left edge of the two-column section.
