Keeps the browser URL query parameters in sync with key calculator state fields (project_type, script_length_seconds, usage_duration, territory, target_budget_usd) and parses them on load to restore or share a specific quote configuration.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 2-column layout likely based on a centered max-width container (approx. 1080–1200px) with a simple percentage-based grid. Overall layout could easily map to a 12-column system (image ~5 columns, audio list ~7 columns).
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom flow: a full-width black header bar with navigation (shared site chrome, not redefined here) → generous vertical gap → a full-width white content area with a centered script-style page title 'Rates & Quotes' → another vertical gap → a main two-column content band. In that band, the left column is dedicated to understanding pricing (rates-overview-section stacked above rate-guides-list), while the right column is dedicated to action (instant-quote-calculator stacked above quote-estimate-display, copy-quote-to-inquiry-button, quote-inquiry-form, and direct-booking-link). Below this main band, the faqs-section sits as a full-width, lower-contrast section. The url-state-sync component is non-visual and lives at the page level, coordinating state between interactive components without occupying visible space.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom flow: a full-width black header bar with navigation (shared site chrome, not redefined here) → generous vertical gap → a full-width white content area with a centered script-style page title 'Rates & Quotes' → another vertical gap → a main two-column content band. In that band, the left column is dedicated to understanding pricing (rates-overview-section stacked above rate-guides-list), while the right column is dedicated to action (instant-quote-calculator stacked above quote-estimate-display, copy-quote-to-inquiry-button, quote-inquiry-form, and direct-booking-link). Below this main band, the faqs-section sits as a full-width, lower-contrast section. The url-state-sync component is non-visual and lives at the page level, coordinating state between interactive components without occupying visible space.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header occupies roughly 12–15% of the viewport height. The main content area is a centered container with a max-width around 1080–1200px and wide side margins (about 15–20% of the viewport width combined left and right). The 'Rates & Quotes' title plus its top/bottom spacing uses about 18–22% of the viewport height, creating a strong focal entry. The remaining space above the fold is dominated by the two-column band: horizontally the left column takes about 40–45% of the content width, the right column about 55–60%. Vertically within this band, the left column’s rates-overview-section occupies the upper half of the left column, with the rate-guides-list filling the lower half; the right column allocates its upper half to the instant-quote-calculator and quote-estimate-display, with the lower half used by the quote-inquiry-form and auxiliary controls. The faqs-section sits below with a clear 48–64px separation from the two-column section and its own tall vertical breathing room.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density stays moderately spacious. Major blocks (page title, two-column band, FAQ section) are separated vertically by 32–64px. Inside each block, internal elements use the 8px-based spacing system: 16–24px padding inside cards and panels; 16–24px vertical gaps between list items, form groups, and stacked elements. The right column’s action components mirror the mockup’s audio player stack: each component feels like a distinct, full-width pill or card with comfortable padding and clear separation, avoiding visual clutter. Text-heavy areas like FAQs are broken into discrete units (questions/answers) with at least 16px of vertical spacing to maintain readability.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow echoes the mockup’s Z-pattern and left-to-right emphasis. The eye first traverses the black header (logo to nav to any icons), then drops to the centered 'Rates & Quotes' title. From there, the gaze moves down and slightly left to the rates-overview-section, where the visitor gains context on pricing. As in the mockup, the key interactive elements form a strong vertical stack on the right: the eye moves from the left overview across to the instant-quote-calculator at the top of the right column, then scans down through the quote-estimate-display, copy-quote-to-inquiry-button, and the quote-inquiry-form. The faqs-section at the bottom provides a slower, more detailed reading path once the primary tasks (understanding rates and getting a quote) are addressed. The url-state-sync operates invisibly, so it does not disrupt this visual flow.

layout.overall_description: The page sits beneath a full-width black header bar that anchors the experience and carries the site-wide navigation. Below this, the canvas opens into a bright white content area, centered within a max-width container so there are generous white margins on both sides. At the top of this area, the 'Rates & Quotes' heading is centered horizontally and given ample vertical spacing above and below, echoing the theatrical, minimal hero treatment from the mockup. Just beneath the title, the content splits into a clean two-column band: the left side is devoted to understanding how pricing works, while the right side is devoted to configuring and submitting a quote. The left column houses a prominent rates overview block followed by a structured list of rate guides, forming a contextual pillar. The right column forms a vertical stack of interaction panels—starting with the instant quote calculator and the live estimate panel, then a clear call-to-action button, and finally a detailed quote inquiry form and a subtle direct booking link—each spanning the full column width like pill-shaped modules, reminiscent of the stacked audio players in the original mockup. Below this main band, a full-width FAQ section runs across the entire content width in a slightly quieter tone, giving the user room to explore details without competing with the primary actions above. Overall, the layout feels spacious and focused, guiding the user from understanding to action with a smooth left-to-right then top-to-bottom flow, while preserving the clean black-and-white aesthetic and theatrical, creative character of the original design.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The rates-overview-section sits in the left column of the main two-column band, directly below the centered 'Rates & Quotes' heading. The main content container is centered on the page and constrained to a max-width of roughly 1080–1200px; within this, the left column occupies about 40–45% of the container’s width. The top edge of the rates-overview-section starts approximately 32–48px below the bottom of the page title, aligning horizontally so its left edge is flush with the left edge of the main content container. The section itself appears as a single large, clean block (e.g., a card-like area) with 16–24px of internal padding on all sides, matching the airy feel of the mockup. Vertically, it takes up roughly the top half of the left column above the fold, leaving room below for the rate-guides-list while still appearing visually dominant. There is a 24–32px vertical gap between the bottom of this section and the start of the rate-guides-list. The right column’s instant-quote-calculator aligns at the same top baseline as this section, so the two form a cohesive horizontal band.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level element within the left column of a responsive two-column layout; it stretches to fill the full width of the left column and reflows above the right column on narrow screens (e.g., below tablet breakpoint), maintaining a 32–48px gap from the title and subsequent components.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The rate-guides-list is placed directly beneath the rates-overview-section in the left column, continuing the vertical stack of informational content. It shares the same left and right boundaries as the rates-overview-section, filling the full width of the left column. There is a consistent 24–32px vertical gap between the bottom of the rates-overview-section and the top of the rate-guides-list, following the same breathing room used between major elements in the mockup. Each item in the data-list is presented as a row or compact card with 12–16px of vertical padding and 16–24px of horizontal padding, stacked vertically with 8–16px gaps between items. The entire list’s height is allowed to grow as needed but is visually balanced so that, at typical desktop heights, its bottom edge roughly aligns with or slightly exceeds the midpoint of the right column’s quote-inquiry-form, creating a balanced vertical rhythm between the two columns. The list’s content remains clearly secondary to the main rates-overview-section but more detailed than the headline, supporting users who want structured reference information before or while they use the calculator.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level element anchored below the rates-overview-section within the left column; it expands vertically based on item count. On narrow screens, it reflows to appear directly below the rates-overview-section and above the right-column components, maintaining 24–32px vertical spacing above and below.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The faqs-section is positioned as a distinct full-width band below the entire two-column content area. Starting roughly 48–64px beneath the lowest element in either column (typically the bottom of the quote-inquiry-form or direct-booking-link), it spans the full width of the centered content container, aligning with the same left and right margins used by the columns. This section is visually separated from the main quote area by generous top padding (32–48px) and may use a subtle change in background tone (e.g., a very light gray) while maintaining the clean black-and-white palette. Within this section, FAQ items are stacked vertically, each question-answer block taking full available width with 16–24px vertical padding and 16–24px gaps between items. Heading text for the section (e.g., 'FAQs') is left-aligned at the same baseline as other headings in the page and sits 16–24px above the first FAQ item. The overall height of this section can extend significantly below the fold, becoming a scrollable depth of information that users reach after engaging with the main rates and quote tools.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static full-width section that anchors below the primary two-column content. It does not scroll independently; instead, it becomes visible as the user scrolls down the page. On narrow screens, it retains full-width behavior, with internal content remaining stacked and centered within the same max-width container.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The instant-quote-calculator occupies the top of the right column in the main two-column band, mirroring the placement of the rates-overview-section on the left. The right column takes about 55–60% of the content container’s width and starts at the same vertical baseline as the rates-overview-section, creating a clean horizontal alignment between columns. The calculator module is presented as a prominent, pill-like card spanning the full width of the right column, with 16–24px padding inside and softly rounded corners, analogous to a large interactive audio player from the mockup. It sits approximately 32–48px below the page title and about 24–32px to the right of the left column (the column gutter). Within the calculator, form controls (dropdowns, sliders, inputs) are arranged in a vertical sequence with 12–16px gaps, ensuring it feels substantial but not cramped. The vertical dimension of this block is significant—roughly 30–40% of the right column’s visible height—so it reads clearly as the main interactive tool at a glance. A 16–24px gap separates the bottom of the calculator from the quote-estimate-display below it.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level element occupying the full width of the right column. On smaller viewports, it reflows to appear below the left column content, but maintains its status as the first major interactive component in the vertical stack with 32–48px spacing from the preceding element.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The quote-estimate-display sits directly beneath the instant-quote-calculator in the right column, forming the second element in the stacked interactive sequence. It spans the full width of the right column and is visually styled as a concise, pill-shaped summary panel with 16–24px of padding and clear typographic hierarchy for the estimated rate. There is a 16–24px vertical gap between the bottom of the calculator and the top of this panel, mirroring the stacked spacing of audio players in the mockup. The panel is not overly tall—typically 15–20% of the right column height—but its contrasting background and bold key figure make it visually prominent. Below the quote-estimate-display, a 16–24px vertical gap leads into the copy-quote-to-inquiry-button, reinforcing the sense that this display is a pivot point from configuration to action. As the user scrolls, this estimate panel tends to stay within view alongside the calculator and form, so users can always see the current estimate as they adjust inputs or prepare to submit an inquiry.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a static block in the normal flow, but on desktop and larger tablet viewports it may use a sticky behavior within the right column: once the user scrolls past its original position, it remains stuck near the top of the right column viewport until the bottom of the column content is reached. It never overlaps the header and respects the internal padding and margins of the main container. On smaller screens, it degrades gracefully to a standard static block without stickiness.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The copy-quote-to-inquiry-button is placed immediately below the quote-estimate-display in the right column, forming a clear connective tissue between viewing an estimate and submitting a detailed inquiry. It is horizontally centered within the right column and typically spans 70–100% of the column’s width, appearing as a wide, pill-shaped button with generous padding (12–16px vertically and 24–32px horizontally). There is a 16–24px vertical gap between the bottom of the quote-estimate-display and the top of the button, maintaining consistency with the vertical rhythm of the stacked players in the original mockup. The button’s label is centered and clearly legible, and its presence visually anchors the transition from numeric estimate to form entry. Below the button, another 16–24px vertical gap introduces the top of the quote-inquiry-form, reinforcing the sense of an ordered, stepwise flow down the right column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static inline-block or block-level element in the right column’s vertical stack. It does not stick or float; it maintains its position between the quote-estimate-display and the quote-inquiry-form. On small screens, it remains between these components in the linear flow, retaining its wide, touch-friendly dimensions.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The quote-inquiry-form is the largest and most vertically extensive component in the right column, placed directly beneath the copy-quote-to-inquiry-button. It spans the full width of the right column, with a 16–24px vertical gap at the top separating it from the button. The form appears as a structured, card-like block with 16–24px of internal padding on all sides and consistent spacing (12–16px) between input fields, textareas, and field groups. Its top edge aligns with the right column’s left edge, creating a clean vertical column of interactive elements from the calculator down. Vertically, the form can extend significantly below the fold, especially on desktop, making the right column taller than the left in most cases; this is acceptable and visually anchored by the card-like styling. The bottom of the form sits about 24–32px above the direct-booking-link, which appears as a distinct, lighter-weight element below. This ordering makes it clear that sending a tailored quote inquiry is the default, thorough option after configuring an estimate.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level element within the right column, expanding vertically as needed based on the number of fields. On smaller screens, it appears below the calculator, estimate display, and copy button in a single column layout, maintaining the same internal spacing and card structure. It scrolls with the page and does not adopt sticky or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The direct-booking-link sits at the very bottom of the right column’s content stack, just below the quote-inquiry-form. It is separated from the form by a modest 24–32px vertical gap, enough to indicate it is a separate, alternative path rather than part of the main form flow. The link is typically rendered as a minimal text link or small button, right-aligned or center-aligned within the right column, occupying only as much horizontal space as its label requires. Its visual weight is intentionally lighter than the primary button and form, so it reads as an optional shortcut for users who already know what they need. The link’s baseline aligns with the right column’s content grid, and its placement ensures that when the user reaches the bottom of the column, they encounter this link as a final, clear call to action. The bottom edge of this link aligns roughly with or just below the lowest content in the left column (rate-guides-list) before the faqs-section begins, maintaining a visually tidy boundary between the two-column band and the FAQ area below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static inline element at the bottom of the right column; it does not stick or float. On smaller viewports where content collapses into a single column, it remains last in the interactive sequence, appearing below the quote-inquiry-form with the same 24–32px top margin.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The url-state-sync component operates behind the scenes and does not produce any visible UI element. Conceptually, it sits at the page or application root level, wrapping or observing the instant-quote-calculator, quote-estimate-display, and quote-inquiry-form. It synchronizes the calculator’s configuration and current estimate with the URL (e.g., query parameters) and shares this state with the form, so that reloading or sharing the page preserves the configured quote. Because it is non-visual, it occupies no actual space in the layout and does not affect the spacing or positioning of any of the visible components. All spacing, margins, and alignments described for other components remain unchanged whether or not this state manager is present.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, structural/state-management component that does not participate in the layout flow. It can be implemented as a wrapper or context provider at the top of the page’s component tree, fixed in a logical rather than spatial sense; it has no width, height, or on-screen position and does not interact with scrolling or responsiveness.

name: Contact & Booking
title: Contact & Booking
description: Page for sending general contact messages and submitting detailed booking requests for voiceover or acting sessions, with support for scheduling, time zones, and remote connection preferences.

core_features: Tabbed interface that switches between a 'General Contact' form and a 'Booking Request' form, with the active tab controlled by the URL parameter 'tab'.
Automatic loading of dynamic introductory copy and general contact information (email, phone, location, office hours) using getContactPageContent.
General contact form that submits non-booking inquiries via submitContactMessage, with inline validation and success/error confirmation messages.
Booking request form that captures session type, session length (hours), date, time, time zone, preferred connection methods, and additional details, submitted via submitBookingRequest.
Inline input validation for both forms (required fields, value formats, logical constraints) and display of interface-returned confirmation or error messages.
Pre-filling of booking form fields from URL parameters (session_type, session_length_hours, session_date, session_time, timezone) to support deep links from other pages or external sources.
Link from the General Contact tab to the rates.html page for viewing rates and quotes.
URL parameter-based state so that links can open directly to the booking tab or a partially preconfigured booking request, without relying on localStorage.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Send a general contact message
1. User navigates to contact_booking.html (typically via header or footer link) without URL parameters.
2. Page controller reads URL; with no 'tab' parameter, it sets the active tab to 'contact'.
3. Page calls getContactPageContent and populates the general contact intro text and contact information block.
4. User reviews the email, phone, location, and office hours, then enters their name, email, optional subject, and message into the General Contact form.
5. On field blur or form change, inline validation runs (e.g., required fields, valid email format) and displays any errors per field.
6. User submits the form; the component calls submitContactMessage with name, email, subject, and message from the form state.
7. On success=true, the form shows a success confirmation, optionally clears or disables the form, and may display the returned contact_message_id in a non-technical way if desired.
8. On success=false, the form displays the returned message as an error banner and keeps the user input so they can retry.

Workflow: Submit a booking request from a deep link
1. A user opens a link such as contact_booking.html?tab=booking&session_type=...&session_length_hours=...&session_date=...&session_time=...&timezone=....
2. Page controller parses the URL parameters and sets the active tab to 'booking'.
3. Page calls getContactPageContent and populates the booking intro text (plus any global contact info needed on this tab).
4. Booking Request form initializes its internal state using the URL parameters when present (e.g., preselects the session_type, sets session_length_hours, and pre-fills date, time, and time zone fields).
5. User reviews and adjusts session type, length, date, time, time zone, and selects one or more preferred connection methods (e.g., Source-Connect, Zoom, or others), and adds any backup times or special notes in the additional details field.
6. Inline validation runs as the user completes fields (e.g., required session_type, positive session_length_hours, valid date/time, non-empty timezone). Any validation issues are shown next to the relevant input.
7. User clicks the 'Submit Booking Request' button; the form component calls submitBookingRequest with session_type, session_length_hours, session_date, session_time, timezone, preferred_connection_methods (collected from checkboxes), and additional_details from state.
8. On success=true, the form displays a confirmation message using the returned message and may show a summary including session_type, session_length_hours, timezone, and status from booking_request. The form fields can be disabled or cleared depending on design.
9. On success=false, the form shows the returned message as an error and keeps the current field values so the user can correct and resubmit.

Workflow: Switch between general contact and booking without losing progress
1. User lands on one tab (e.g., General Contact) and starts filling out the form.
2. User clicks the Booking tab in the tab switcher.
3. The tab switcher component updates the URL parameter 'tab' to 'booking' (e.g., contact_booking.html?tab=booking plus any existing non-conflicting parameters) using the history API.
4. The page switches the visible panel to the Booking Request form but keeps the in-memory state of the General Contact form so that if the user switches back, their partially completed fields remain.
5. User can fill out the Booking Request form and then switch back to General Contact; the same URL parameter update occurs (tab=contact), and the Booking form state is also preserved in memory until the page is reloaded.

Workflow: Navigate from General Contact to Rates & Quotes
1. User views the General Contact tab and decides they need pricing information.
2. User clicks the 'View rates & quotes' link in the General Contact panel.
3. The browser navigates to rates.html with no additional parameters (as specified in outgoing navigation for this page).
4. If the user later returns to contact_booking.html (e.g., via browser back or navigation), the page reinitializes, again reading URL parameters and restoring default or parameter-driven state.

Workflow: External casting director preps a booking
1. Casting director receives an external link pointing to the Booking Request tab with certain fields preconfigured (e.g., from voiceover.html or acting.html).
2. They open the link; the page reads URL parameters including tab=booking, session_type, and possibly session_length_hours, session_date, session_time, timezone.
3. The Booking tab becomes active, and the form pre-fills fields with the provided values.
4. Casting director selects connection methods and adds project-specific notes or backup time slots in additional_details.
5. They submit the Booking Request, and the system responds with a confirmation message and a pending status via submitBookingRequest.
interactions: Tab click: When the user clicks the 'General Contact' or 'Booking Request' tab in the tab switcher, the active tab state changes, the URL parameter 'tab' is updated accordingly, and the corresponding panel is rendered.

Page load: On initial load or on URL change, the page controller parses URL parameters (tab, session_type, session_length_hours, session_date, session_time, timezone) to determine which tab is active and how to prefill the Booking Request form.
API initialization: On page load, the controller calls getContactPageContent and binds general_contact_intro_html, booking_intro_html, and general_contact_info fields to the relevant components.

Contact form field input: As the user types or changes values in the General Contact form fields (name, email, subject, message), the form updates its internal state and runs per-field validation on blur or on input, displaying inline error messages when needed.

Contact form submission: On submit of the General Contact form, the component prevents default browser submission, checks client-side validation, and then calls submitContactMessage with the form values. The UI shows a loading state until the API responds.

Contact form result handling: After submitContactMessage returns, the component reads success and message. On success, it shows a success banner and may clear or lock the form. On failure, it shows an error banner and retains form values.

Booking form field input: As the user modifies booking fields (session_type, session_length_hours, date, time, timezone, connection method checkboxes, additional_details), the form updates its state and performs inline validation (e.g., required fields, numeric ranges).

Booking form submission: On click of 'Submit Booking Request', the form validates all required fields. If valid, it calls submitBookingRequest with the current state, including preferred_connection_methods as an array of selected identifiers.

Booking form result handling: After submitBookingRequest returns, the form displays the returned message and, when success is true, may show a simple summary from booking_request (status, session_start, etc.). It handles error responses by showing an error banner and preserving form inputs.
Rates link click: When the user clicks the 'View rates & quotes' link on the General Contact tab, the browser navigates to rates.html.

Error display: If getContactPageContent fails or returns incomplete data, the page controller and individual components show fallback error messages or hide dependent sections while still allowing form usage where possible (no static data is substituted).
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary page state is controlled via URL parameters and transient in-memory component state. On page initialization, the controller parses the 'tab' parameter. If tab=booking, the Booking tab is made active; otherwise, the General Contact tab is active by default. The URL parameter 'tab' is updated when the user switches tabs so that the current tab can be restored on reload or when sharing the URL. For the Booking Request form, the URL parameters session_type, session_length_hours, session_date, session_time, and timezone are inspected once during initialization. If these parameters are present and valid, they are used to pre-populate the corresponding fields in the booking form's internal state; they do not automatically update in response to subsequent field edits (i.e., form changes do not rewrite those particular URL parameters, unless explicitly implemented). In-memory component state (not localStorage) stores current form inputs and validation errors for both forms so that switching tabs does not clear user input. No persistent client-side storage is used beyond the URL, ensuring that all shareable or bookmarkable state is expressed via query parameters while form-interaction and validation state is ephemeral.

components.functionality: Initializes the Contact & Booking page, loads dynamic content via getContactPageContent, parses URL parameters to determine the active tab and any booking prefill values, and coordinates data flow between the tab switcher, forms, and global notifications.
Provides two selectable tabs, 'General Contact' and 'Booking Request'. Controls which panel is visible and updates the 'tab' URL parameter when the user switches tabs.

Displays general contact introduction text and dynamic contact details (email, phone, location, office hours) returned by getContactPageContent. Provides the 'View rates & quotes' navigation link to rates.html.

Collects non-booking inquiries including name, email, optional subject, and message. Performs inline validation and submits data via submitContactMessage, then displays success or error feedback based on the API response.

Captures and manages all booking request details: session_type selection (pre-filled from URL when provided), session_length_hours, session_date, session_time, timezone, preferred_connection_methods (multi-select), and additional_details. Applies inline validation rules and sends a booking request via submitBookingRequest, then presents the confirmation or error message along with returned booking_request details.

Displays contextual status messages (success or error) for both the General Contact and Booking Request forms, driven by the success and message values returned from submitContactMessage and submitBookingRequest. Can be instantiated per form to encapsulate its messages.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 2-column layout likely based on a centered max-width container (approx. 1080–1200px) with a simple percentage-based grid. Overall layout could easily map to a 12-column system (image ~5 columns, audio list ~7 columns).
layout.layout_pattern: The page keeps the mockup’s clear top-to-bottom structure: a full-width black site header (handled globally) at the very top, followed by a centered white main content area. Inside the main area, the first visible element is a large, centered page title ('Contact & Booking') with generous spacing above and below, echoing the 'Acting' heading treatment. Beneath this, the core content is arranged in a two-column layout: the left column holds supporting contact information, while the right column holds the interactive controls (tab switcher) and the stacked forms, mirroring the image-left / audio-list-right pattern in the mockup.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page keeps the mockup’s clear top-to-bottom structure: a full-width black site header (handled globally) at the very top, followed by a centered white main content area. Inside the main area, the first visible element is a large, centered page title ('Contact & Booking') with generous spacing above and below, echoing the 'Acting' heading treatment. Beneath this, the core content is arranged in a two-column layout: the left column holds supporting contact information, while the right column holds the interactive controls (tab switcher) and the stacked forms, mirroring the image-left / audio-list-right pattern in the mockup.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The global black header bar occupies roughly the top 12–15% of the viewport height. The main content area below spans a centered band about 60–70% of the viewport width, with 15–20% combined left/right margins for breathing room. Within this band, ~20–25% of the vertical space is dedicated to the page title block and its surrounding whitespace. The remaining vertical space is used by the two-column section, where the left column (info block) occupies about 40–45% of the content width and the right column (tabs + forms + status) about 55–60%, separated by a narrow gutter (~4–6% of the content width). Vertical spacing between major blocks (title to columns, columns to any subsequent sections) follows a 32–64px rhythm based on the 8px unit.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout maintains a moderately spacious feel: no dense text slabs, no cramped fields. The info block uses concise copy with plenty of whitespace around it. Within the right column, the tab switcher and forms are grouped into clean, pill-like sections with 16–24px internal padding and 16–24px vertical gaps between field groups. There is consistent vertical spacing (16–24px) between the tab switcher, status messages, and the forms, producing a clear visual hierarchy and easy scanning, similar to the stacked audio players in the mockup.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow mirrors the mockup’s Z-pattern: users first scan left-to-right across the black header (logo → navigation → icons), then their eye is drawn down to the large centered 'Contact & Booking' heading. From there, the gaze drops slightly left to the general contact info block, which acts like the visual anchor (parallel to the portrait image in the mockup). Next, attention tracks horizontally to the right column where the tab switcher is the primary interactive entry point. From the tabs, the eye moves downward through the notification banner (when present) and then vertically down the active form fields, creating a strong top-to-bottom scanning flow in the right column analogous to the stacked audio players.

layout.overall_description: Below the fixed black site header, the page opens into a white canvas with a wide, centered content band. At the top of this band, the title 'Contact & Booking' sits prominently in the center, styled like a key section heading with ample white space above and below so it feels theatrical and intentional rather than crowded. Dropping below this heading, the layout splits into two columns. On the left, a clean, card-like info block about contacting and booking sits in a rounded rectangle, occupying a bit less than half of the width and aligning roughly with where the hero image appears in the original mockup. On the right, taking up the remaining width, a horizontal tab switcher lets visitors toggle between a general contact form and a detailed booking request form. Directly beneath the tabs, a subtle but noticeable notification banner space is reserved for success/error messages, followed by the active form rendered as a tall, pill-shaped panel with stacked fields. The result is a calm, centered layout where the left side quickly orients the visitor, and the right side leads them through a clear, vertical interaction flow that echoes the original image-and-audio composition: left for context, right for action.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page controller defines the main content area directly beneath the global black header bar (which remains fixed at the top and is not part of this component). It creates a centered content container with a max width of approximately 1080–1200px, aligned in the middle of the viewport with equal left and right margins, each representing roughly 15–20% of the viewport width depending on screen size. Inside this container, it first places the 'Contact & Booking' page heading in the center, with 48–64px of vertical space separating it from both the bottom of the header and the start of the two-column section below. Under the heading, the controller establishes a two-column grid: the left column spans roughly 5 out of 12 notional grid columns (about 40–45% of the content width), and the right column spans the remaining 7 out of 12 columns (about 55–60%), with a 24–32px gutter between them. The vertical spacing between this main two-column band and any adjacent sections is kept generous (32–48px), preserving the airy feel from the mockup. On smaller viewports, the controller stacks these columns vertically in a single column flow: title at the top, then the info block, then the tab switcher and forms. The controller itself scrolls normally with the page and has no fixed or sticky behavior.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Acts as the main layout container below the fixed global header, constraining content width and orchestrating the two-column grid. It scrolls with the page, does not stick, and provides consistent horizontal centering and vertical spacing for all child components.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The tab switcher is positioned at the top of the right-hand column established by the page controller. It aligns flush with the top edge of the general-contact-info-block on the left, so that the left and right columns appear to start at the same vertical baseline. Horizontally, the tab switcher spans the full width of the right column (roughly 55–60% of the central content band). It is rendered as a low-profile horizontal pill bar containing two or three tabs (e.g., 'General Contact' and 'Booking Request'), with each tab given 16–24px horizontal padding and 8–12px vertical padding, reflecting the airy button sizing in the mockup. There is approximately 16px of vertical space above the tab switcher to separate it from the bottom of the page title area, and 12–16px of vertical space below it before any status messages or form content. The active tab’s visual style should be slightly more prominent (e.g., filled pill or underlined), but the overall component remains slender so it feels like a selector rather than a heavy section header. On narrow screens, the tab switcher continues to span the full width of the single column, remaining at the top of the form area.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Sits in normal document flow at the top of the right column and scrolls with the page. It does not stick to the top of the viewport; instead, it remains visually connected to the forms it controls. Switching tabs swaps which form is rendered in the right column content area directly beneath it.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The general contact info block occupies the left column of the main two-column layout, mirroring the position and vertical emphasis of the portrait image in the original mockup. It starts at the same vertical level as the tab switcher in the right column, so their top edges line up horizontally. This block is presented as a vertically oriented card or panel with a light background (white) and subtle border or shadow, optionally with slightly rounded corners to echo the rounded rectangle shape seen in the mockup’s image component. It spans roughly 40–45% of the content width, with 24–32px of internal padding on all sides. The content inside is arranged top-to-bottom: a short heading (e.g., 'Get in touch'), a few lines of explanatory text, and key contact details (email, social links, maybe an alternate booking agency mention), with 8–16px vertical spacing between text elements. The bottom of this block ends roughly in line with the upper half of the form area on the right, maintaining a sense of balance rather than extending all the way down. There is about 24–32px of space between the outer edges of this block and the container edges: a 24–32px gutter to the right (between this block and the right column) and comfortable white space to the left inside the main content band. On mobile or narrow screens, this info block appears full-width directly below the title and above the tab switcher and forms.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Remains a static card within the left column, scrolling naturally with the rest of the content. It does not resize dynamically beyond standard responsive width adjustments (switching from a column card on desktop to a full-width block in stacked mobile layouts).

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The general contact form occupies the main content area of the right column when the corresponding tab in the contact-booking-tab-switcher is active. It appears directly beneath any visible form-status-messages component, or if there is no current status message, directly beneath the tab switcher with a 16–24px vertical gap. Horizontally, the form spans the full width of the right column, rendered as a tall, pill-like panel with slightly rounded corners and a subtle border or shadow, similar in visual weight to the audio player stack in the mockup but larger and more vertically expansive. Inside this panel, fields such as name, email, subject, and message are arranged in a single vertical column, each label–input pair separated by 16–24px of vertical spacing. The panel uses 24–32px of internal padding on all sides to maintain the airy feel. The submit button is placed at the bottom right of the panel, aligned with the right edge of the input fields, with 16–24px of vertical padding above it. The overall height of this form is moderate, ending comfortably above the bottom of the viewport on most screens, so the entire form often fits without vertical scrolling. On smaller screens, it still appears directly under the tab switcher and any status message, expanding full-width in the single-column layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Exists in the same spatial slot as the booking-request-form, with only one form visible at a time based on the active tab. It scrolls with the page and resizes responsively to the width of the right column or full width on mobile, but otherwise has no fixed or sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The booking request form shares the same spatial position as the general-contact-form within the right column, appearing in its place when the 'Booking' tab is active in the tab switcher. It is anchored directly below the form-status-messages component (or the tab switcher if no status message is currently displayed), with a 16–24px vertical gap. The form spans the full width of the right column and is styled as a tall, rounded panel similar to the general-contact-form, maintaining consistent visual language. Because it captures more detailed information (project type, preferred dates and times, time zone, remote connection preferences, script details, etc.), this form is taller and may extend below the initial viewport height, requiring vertical scrolling. Internally, fields are grouped logically into sections (e.g., project details, scheduling, technical setup), with 16–24px spacing between groups and 8–16px between individual fields. Each section heading is left-aligned within the panel and separated by a slightly larger vertical gap (24px) to act as clear anchors in the vertical flow, analogous to the way individual audio players break up the right column in the mockup. The submit button sits at the bottom right of the form, with generous padding and at least 24px of whitespace beneath it before the bottom edge of the panel. On small screens, the form stretches full-width beneath the tab switcher and status messages, stacking fields vertically in a single column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Occupies the same right-column content region as the general-contact-form, with mutual exclusivity controlled by the tab switcher. It scrolls with the page and can extend beyond the viewport height, encouraging vertical scanning similar to the stacked audio list in the mockup, but has no sticky or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The form status messages component is positioned within the right column, directly beneath the contact-booking-tab-switcher and above whichever form (general-contact-form or booking-request-form) is currently active. When rendered, it spans the full width of the right column, forming a slim horizontal banner that visually sits between the tabs and the form content, much like a narrow strip of feedback in the path of interaction. There is a 8–12px gap between the bottom of the tab switcher and the top of this banner, and another 12–16px gap between the banner and the top edge of the form panel below. The banner height is modest (roughly 40–56px depending on text), with 12–16px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding, enough room for an icon and one or two lines of text. Color can be used here (e.g. a subtle green or red) to signal success or errors without overwhelming the primarily black-and-white aesthetic. When no status needs to be shown, this component collapses entirely and the active form moves up to sit closer to the tab switcher, preserving the clean vertical rhythm. On mobile, the banner continues to occupy the full width of the single column, maintaining its position between the tabs and form content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears and disappears in normal flow within the right column, expanding or collapsing vertically without overlapping other components. It always maintains its position between the tab switcher and the active form, and scrolls along with the page.

name: Clients & Testimonials
title: Clients & Testimonials
description: Browse testimonials by client type and year, expand individual testimonials, and manage a list of favorite clients, with all state controlled by URL parameters.

core_features: Load and display page introduction content and client type descriptions using getClientsTestimonialsPageContent.
Display a filter bar for client type and testimonial year range and a sort selector, with options loaded from getTestimonialFilterOptions.
Respect incoming URL parameters (client_type, min_year, max_year, sort_by, view, page, page_size) to initialize filters, selected view, sorting, and pagination.
Provide two main views controlled by the view URL parameter: an 'All Testimonials' view (view=all) and a 'Favorite Clients' view (view=favorites).
In the All Testimonials view, list testimonials returned by searchTestimonials, each showing a short quote preview with an expandable 'Read more' section for the full testimonial.
Within each testimonial’s expanded view, allow the user to mark the associated client as a favorite using addClientToFavorites, updating the favorite status in the UI and the favorites counter.
In the Favorite Clients view, list favorite clients returned by getFavoriteClients, including their latest testimonial context, and provide sorting using sort options passed to getFavoriteClients.
Provide a favorites counter and tab switcher so users can switch between All Testimonials and Favorite Clients, with the active tab reflected in the URL’s view parameter.
Offer a 'Contact' or 'Work with me' navigation control that links to contact_booking.html with the tab=contact parameter from both the intro area and the Favorites view.
Support changing filters, view, sort, and pagination via URL updates so that the current state can be shared or reloaded consistently.
user_workflows:
Arriving on the page without URL parameters:
1. User navigates to clients_testimonials.html from site navigation.
2. The page-init component calls getClientsTestimonialsPageContent to load intro_html and client_type_descriptions.
3. The page-init component calls getTestimonialFilterOptions to load client_types, year_range, and sort_options.
4. Since there are no URL parameters, the page sets defaults (e.g., view=all, sort_by based on a default sort option from getTestimonialFilterOptions, min_year and max_year from year_range), and updates the URL accordingly.
5. The testimonials-section component calls searchTestimonials with the derived client_type (if any), min_year, max_year, sort_by, and default pagination (page and page_size) to load the first page of testimonials.
6. Results are rendered as a list of testimonial previews in the All Testimonials view.

Arriving on the page with pre-filtered URL parameters (e.g., from an external link):
1. User opens a URL like clients_testimonials.html?client_type={id}&min_year={year}&view=all.
2. The page-init component still calls getClientsTestimonialsPageContent and getTestimonialFilterOptions.
3. The filter-bar component reads client_type and year parameters from the URL and sets its initial selections accordingly.
4. The testimonials-section component calls searchTestimonials with client_type and the year range derived from min_year and max_year URL parameters, plus sort_by if provided.
5. The testimonial list displays only the testimonials matching the specified client type and year range.

Expanding a testimonial to read the full quote:
1. In the All Testimonials view, the user clicks a 'Read more' control on a testimonial card.
2. The testimonial-item component toggles an internal expanded state for that testimonial and reveals quote_full along with additional testimonial context (project_description, contact_name, etc.).
3. If the user clicks 'Read less' or the same toggle again, the testimonial-item collapses back to showing only quote_short.

Marking a client as a favorite from a testimonial:
1. While a testimonial is expanded, the user clicks a 'Add to Favorites' or favorite icon control.
2. The testimonial-item component triggers addClientToFavorites with the testimonial’s client_id.
3. On success, the component updates the testimonial’s is_favorite_client state to true and may display a confirmation message from the response.
4. The favorites-tab component updates its displayed favorites count based on favorites_total from the addClientToFavorites response.
5. If the user is currently in the Favorite Clients view, a subsequent refresh of that view (via getFavoriteClients) will include the newly favorited client.

Viewing and sorting favorite clients:
1. User clicks the 'Favorite Clients' tab in the tab-switcher.
2. The tab-switcher updates the URL to set view=favorites and may reset or preserve sort_by according to the current selection.
3. The favorites-section component calls getFavoriteClients with the current sort_by (taken from the URL or a default).
4. The favorite-clients-list component renders each favorite client with company_name, client_type_label, the latest testimonial preview, and added_at information.
5. The user changes the favorites sort option via the favorites-sort-control component (e.g., selecting a sort option whose id sorts by client name ascending, as provided by getTestimonialFilterOptions.sort_options or by a dedicated configuration for favorites).
6. The favorites-sort-control updates the sort_by parameter in the URL and triggers a new getFavoriteClients call with the updated sort_by value.
7. The Favorite Clients list re-renders in the new order.

Adjusting filters and sorting in the All Testimonials view:
1. User is on view=all. The filter-bar shows current client_type and year range based on URL.
2. The user changes the client_type selection.
3. The filter-bar updates the client_type URL parameter and resets the page parameter to the first page.
4. The testimonials-section responds to URL changes by calling searchTestimonials with the new client_type and current year range and sort_by.
5. The user adjusts the year range (min_year and/or max_year) and the same re-fetch process occurs.
6. The user changes the sort selection. The filter-bar updates sort_by in the URL and triggers a new searchTestimonials call with the new sort order.
7. If pagination controls are used, the pagination-control updates the page parameter in the URL and calls searchTestimonials with that page.

Navigating to contact/booking from this page:
1. In either view, the user clicks a 'Contact' or 'Work with me' control.
2. A navigation is triggered to contact_booking.html?tab=contact, without altering any testimonial or favorites state on this page.
3. If the user later navigates back to clients_testimonials.html using browser navigation, the URL parameters preserved in the history restore their previous filters and view.

Landing on the page directly in the Favorite Clients view via URL:
1. User opens clients_testimonials.html?view=favorites&sort_by={id}.
2. The page-init component calls getClientsTestimonialsPageContent and getTestimonialFilterOptions.
3. The tab-switcher detects view=favorites and activates the Favorite Clients tab.
4. The favorites-section component calls getFavoriteClients with sort_by from the URL.
5. The Favorite Clients list is rendered, and the user may change sorting or navigate back to All Testimonials via the tab-switcher, which updates view in the URL.
interactions:
On page load:
- Trigger: DOMContentLoaded or equivalent.
- Behavior: Read URL parameters (client_type, min_year, max_year, sort_by, view, page, page_size). Call getClientsTestimonialsPageContent and getTestimonialFilterOptions. Initialize all controls and views according to URL-derived state and loaded options.
- Interfaces used: getClientsTestimonialsPageContent, getTestimonialFilterOptions, searchTestimonials (if view=all or unspecified), getFavoriteClients (if view=favorites).

Client type filter change in All Testimonials view:
- Trigger: User selects a different client type from a dropdown or similar input.
- Behavior: Update the client_type parameter in the URL; reset page to the first page; call searchTestimonials with updated client_type and existing min_year, max_year, sort_by, page_size.
- Interfaces used: searchTestimonials.

Year range filter change in All Testimonials view:
- Trigger: User changes minimum and/or maximum year filter control.
- Behavior: Update min_year and/or max_year parameters in the URL; reset page to the first page; call searchTestimonials with updated year range and existing client_type, sort_by, page_size.
- Interfaces used: searchTestimonials.

Sort option change in All Testimonials view:
- Trigger: User selects a different sort option from the sort selector.
- Behavior: Update sort_by in the URL; reset page to the first page; call searchTestimonials with updated sort_by and existing client_type, min_year, max_year, page_size.
- Interfaces used: searchTestimonials.

Pagination control in All Testimonials view:
- Trigger: User clicks next/previous page or a specific page number.
- Behavior: Update page (and optionally page_size) in the URL; call searchTestimonials with existing client_type, min_year, max_year, sort_by, and the new page values.
- Interfaces used: searchTestimonials.

Tab switch between All Testimonials and Favorite Clients:
- Trigger: User clicks the tab for 'All Testimonials' or 'Favorite Clients'.
- Behavior: Update view URL parameter to 'all' or 'favorites'; when switching to 'all', call searchTestimonials with current or default filters; when switching to 'favorites', call getFavoriteClients with current or default sort_by.
- Interfaces used: searchTestimonials, getFavoriteClients.

Expand/collapse testimonial ('Read more' / 'Read less'):
- Trigger: User clicks on a 'Read more' control in a testimonial item.
- Behavior: Toggle a local expanded state for that testimonial item instance. No interface calls are required because quote_full is already available from searchTestimonials.
- Interfaces used: None (uses data already loaded by searchTestimonials).

Mark client as favorite from a testimonial:
- Trigger: User clicks 'Add to Favorites' or a favorite icon within an expanded testimonial.
- Behavior: Call addClientToFavorites with clientId corresponding to the testimonial’s client_id. On success, update the is_favorite_client flag for that testimonial in the UI and update a favorites counter using favorites_total from the response. Optionally refresh the Favorite Clients view if currently visible by calling getFavoriteClients.
- Interfaces used: addClientToFavorites, optionally getFavoriteClients.

Sorting favorites in Favorite Clients view:
- Trigger: User selects a different sort option specifically for favorites.
- Behavior: Update sort_by in the URL; call getFavoriteClients with the new sort_by; update the favorite-clients-list with the sorted results.
- Interfaces used: getFavoriteClients.

Refresh Favorite Clients view after returning to the page:
- Trigger: User navigates back to clients_testimonials.html with view=favorites in the URL.
- Behavior: Read view and sort_by from URL; call getFavoriteClients to repopulate the favorites list; ensure the favorites counter aligns with total_results.
- Interfaces used: getFavoriteClients.

Click on 'Contact' / 'Work with me' action:
- Trigger: User clicks a contact-related control on the page.
- Behavior: Navigate to contact_booking.html?tab=contact, preserving browser history of the current page and its URL parameters.
- Interfaces used: None (navigation only).
page_functionality.state_logic:
The page state is fully driven by URL parameters and responses from the assigned interfaces. The following parameters and logic apply:

- view:
  - Accepts 'all' or 'favorites'.
  - If view is missing or invalid, default logically to 'all' and reflect this in the URL.
  - When view='all', the page shows the All Testimonials view and calls searchTestimonials with filters derived from other URL parameters.
  - When view='favorites', the page shows the Favorite Clients view and calls getFavoriteClients with sort_by from the URL.

- client_type:
  - Accepts a client type identifier matching ids returned from getTestimonialFilterOptions.client_types.
  - Used only when view='all'.
  - On page load, if client_type is present in the URL and matches a valid client_types id, the filter-bar initializes the selection accordingly and passes this client_type to searchTestimonials.
  - If client_type is missing, the filter-bar defaults to a logical 'all types' state (no client_type parameter passed to searchTestimonials) and may remove client_type from the URL.
  - When the user changes client type, the new selection updates the client_type parameter in the URL and triggers a new searchTestimonials call.

- min_year and max_year:
  - Both map to numeric year values within the year_range returned from getTestimonialFilterOptions.year_range.
  - On page load, if min_year and/or max_year exist in the URL and are within the allowed range, they are applied to both the filter controls and searchTestimonials call.
  - If they are missing or out of range, the system derives default min_year and max_year from getTestimonialFilterOptions.year_range and updates the URL to reflect those defaults, if desired.
  - When the user adjusts year filters, the URL parameters (min_year and/or max_year) are updated, pagination (page) is reset, and searchTestimonials is called with the new range.

- sort_by:
  - Accepts a sort identifier that matches one of the sort_options.id values from getTestimonialFilterOptions.sort_options (for testimonials) or an equivalent configuration for favorites.
  - When view='all', sort_by is passed to searchTestimonials. If sort_by is missing or invalid, a default sort option is determined by the page logic (e.g., the first or a specially flagged option from sort_options) and optionally written into the URL.
  - When view='favorites', sort_by is passed to getFavoriteClients to determine the order of favorite clients.
  - Changing sort controls in either view updates sort_by in the URL and triggers the corresponding interface call.

- page and page_size (for All Testimonials view):
  - These parameters are used to control pagination of searchTestimonials results, matching the page and page_size parameters in the searchTestimonials interface.
  - If absent, default values are assumed internally (e.g., page=1 and a system-defined page_size) and may be written back into the URL for shareability.
  - When the user interacts with pagination controls, page (and optionally page_size if user-adjustable) is updated in the URL and searchTestimonials is called with the updated values.
  - When filters (client_type, min_year, max_year, sort_by) change, page is reset to the first page.

- Interplay between view and sort_by:
  - The same URL parameter sort_by is used for both All Testimonials and Favorite Clients but interpreted by different interfaces.
  - When switching views, the system may preserve the existing sort_by; if the value is not meaningful in the target view, the page logic selects an appropriate default for that view and updates the URL accordingly.

All state transitions (filter changes, view switching, sort adjustments, pagination) update the URL parameters so that reloading the page or sharing the URL will reconstruct the same state by re-calling the appropriate interfaces with the URL-derived parameters.

components.functionality: Initializes the Clients & Testimonials page by reading URL parameters, loading intro content, loading filter and sort options, and triggering the initial data fetch for either All Testimonials or Favorite Clients.

Displays the introductory HTML content and any explanatory text about client types retrieved from getClientsTestimonialsPageContent. Provides a 'Contact' or 'Work with me' action that navigates to contact_booking.html?tab=contact.
Shows a list of client type labels and descriptions to help users understand each segment, using client_type_descriptions data.
Allows users to toggle between 'All Testimonials' and 'Favorite Clients' views, reflecting and updating the view URL parameter. Displays the total count of favorite clients when available.

Provides controls for selecting client type and testimonial year range, and selecting a sort option for testimonials. It uses options from getTestimonialFilterOptions and keeps its state synchronized with URL parameters.

Encapsulates the All Testimonials view. Listens to filter and pagination-related URL parameter changes, calls searchTestimonials accordingly, and passes the resulting testimonial data to the testimonial-list and pagination-control components.
Renders a list of testimonials returned from searchTestimonials, each as a testimonial-item, based on the current filters, sort, and pagination.

Displays an individual testimonial with quote_short, client and project details, and a control to expand to quote_full. Shows the favorite status of the client and provides a control to mark the client as a favorite.
Encapsulates the Favorite Clients view. When view=favorites, calls getFavoriteClients with the current sort_by parameter and sends the results to the favorite-clients-list component.

Provides sorting controls specifically for the Favorite Clients view, allowing the user to re-order the favorites based on sort options (e.g., by company name) supported by getFavoriteClients and associated configuration.
Displays the list of favorite clients returned by getFavoriteClients, including company_name, client_type_label, latest_testimonial_quote_short, and latest_testimonial_year.
Controls pagination for the All Testimonials view by updating the page (and optionally page_size) URL parameters and triggering new searchTestimonials calls.
Displays the total number of favorite clients, using favorites_total from addClientToFavorites responses and total_results from getFavoriteClients.
Provides a reusable control that, when activated, navigates the user to contact_booking.html with the tab=contact parameter.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 2-column layout likely based on a centered max-width container (approx. 1080–1200px) with a simple percentage-based grid. Overall layout could easily map to a 12-column system (image ~5 columns, audio list ~7 columns).
layout.layout_pattern: Maintain the mockup’s structure: a full-width black header (existing globally, not part of this spec) remains fixed at the top. Beneath it, the main content sits in a centered container (~1080–1200px max width) with generous side margins. The page title 'Clients & Testimonials' is centered as a standalone heading, followed by a two-column layout. The left column occupies roughly 40–45% of the content width and is used for introductory/contextual information (intro-content, client-type-legend, contact-link, plus a subtle visual emphasis on the legend). The right column occupies roughly 55–60% and carries the main interactive content: tab-switcher, filter-bar, testimonials-section (with testimonial-list, testimonial-item, pagination-control), and the favorites-section (favorites-sort-control, favorite-clients-list, favorites-counter). All components follow a top-to-bottom flow inside these columns.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Maintain the mockup’s structure: a full-width black header (existing globally, not part of this spec) remains fixed at the top. Beneath it, the main content sits in a centered container (~1080–1200px max width) with generous side margins. The page title 'Clients & Testimonials' is centered as a standalone heading, followed by a two-column layout. The left column occupies roughly 40–45% of the content width and is used for introductory/contextual information (intro-content, client-type-legend, contact-link, plus a subtle visual emphasis on the legend). The right column occupies roughly 55–60% and carries the main interactive content: tab-switcher, filter-bar, testimonials-section (with testimonial-list, testimonial-item, pagination-control), and the favorites-section (favorites-sort-control, favorite-clients-list, favorites-counter). All components follow a top-to-bottom flow inside these columns.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header continues to occupy roughly 12–15% of viewport height. The hero title area (page heading plus top and bottom spacing) takes about 20–25% of the remaining above-the-fold area on typical laptop screens. The two-column content fills the rest, with the right column’s testimonial and favorites sections consuming the majority of vertical space. Major vertical separations (header-to-title, title-to-two-column section, between large sections like testimonials and favorites) use 32–64px spacing. Components within sections (filters, lists, controls) use 8–24px spacing, retaining the airy feel. The left column is shorter and more compact (intro, legend, CTA), while the right column is scroll-heavier with the data lists.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content remains moderately spacious. Each major block (intro, legend, filters, testimonial and favorites sections) has clear breathing room with consistent padding (16–24px horizontal, 8–16px vertical inside cards, pills, and controls). Lists (testimonials and favorites) are stacked vertically with consistent gaps, no cramped multi-column tables. Text lines in intro and testimonials use comfortable line length (around 60–75 characters). There is a clear prioritization of a few interactive controls (tab-switcher, filters, favorite actions) rather than dense UI.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Visual flow echoes the mockup: the user first scans left-to-right across the dark header, then drops to the centered page title. From there, the eye moves slightly down and left to the intro content, establishing context and showing the client-type legend. The gaze then shifts to the right column where the tab-switcher and filter-bar invite interaction, flowing down through the vertically stacked testimonials (testimonial-list) and finally to the favorites-section. The pattern is a hybrid Z to F: a Z across the header and title to the two-column split, then an F-shaped reading pattern within the right column as the user scans filters and list items from top to bottom.

layout.overall_description: Below the full-width black header, the main content area appears as a bright white band centered on the page, framed by wide left and right margins. At the top of this band, the page title 'Clients & Testimonials' is centered, rendered with a slightly theatrical, expressive type treatment to echo the creative brand. A generous vertical gap separates the title from the main two-column section. On the left, a relatively narrow column presents a clean block of introductory copy, followed by a neatly framed legend of client types, and a prominent contact link styled as a pill-shaped button. On the right, a wider column begins with a horizontal tab switcher and a compact filter bar below it, both stretching the column’s width. Beneath these, a white, subtly outlined testimonials section holds a vertical stack of testimonial cards, each spanning the full width of the column and separated by consistent whitespace, ending with pagination controls aligned to the bottom-right. Further down in the same right column, a clearly delineated favorites section mirrors that structure in miniature, with a small header row showing the favorites title, a count badge, and sort controls, and a concise list of favorite clients below. The entire layout feels clean and balanced, with the left column framing and explaining the content while the right column houses the core interactive browsing experience, all aligned to an invisible grid that keeps elements orderly and harmonious.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page-init-loader sits on top of all content as a full-viewport overlay when the page first loads or when URL parameters trigger a full state change. It covers the entire browser window edge-to-edge in both width and height, slightly dimming the interface beneath with a translucent charcoal or very dark gray layer. Centered both horizontally and vertically within this overlay is a simple, minimal loader (such as a circular spinner or subtle animated icon) sized at roughly 48–64px, preserving the clean aesthetic. The overlay respects the header but visually sits above it, so the user sees only the loader and dimmed background until initialization is complete.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed-position overlay anchored to all four viewport edges; remains centered and blocks interaction until initialization completes, then fades out smoothly to reveal the main layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The intro-content block is positioned in the left column of the main content area, directly below the centered page title. After the title, a vertical gap of about 32–48px separates it from the top of the two-column grid. The intro-content aligns with the left edge of the content container, spanning approximately 40–45% of the container’s width. It appears as clean, left-aligned body text with a simple heading and a short paragraph or two, no heavy borders, just ample white space around the text. It has internal padding of roughly 16–24px from the container’s side edges, but sits visually as part of the column rather than as a boxed card. There is a 24px vertical gap between the bottom of the intro-content and the client-type-legend below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level element that participates in the two-column grid; scrolls normally with the page and anchors the top of the left column visually.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The client-type-legend is placed directly below the intro-content within the left column, separated by about 24px of vertical space. It occupies the full width of the left column and reads as a compact, lightly framed card: a white background with perhaps a subtle 1px light gray border and 16–24px of padding on all sides. Inside, the legend items are arranged vertically or in a loose grid, each row showing a small colored dot or icon to the left and the client type label to the right. There is a consistent 8–12px vertical gap between legend items. The legend’s overall height is moderate, roughly 1/3 to 1/2 of the column’s above-the-fold height, leaving room beneath for the contact-link. A larger vertical margin of 32–40px separates the bottom of the legend from the contact-link, helping the legend feel like a self-contained visual reference.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, card-like element aligned to the left column; scrolls with the page and does not stick or float.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The tab-switcher sits at the very top of the right column, horizontally aligned roughly with the vertical start of the intro-content in the left column. After the centered page title, the two-column grid begins; in that right-side area, the tab-switcher spans the full width of the right column (about 55–60% of the main content width). It is a low-profile row of pill-like tabs with rounded edges; the active tab is rendered in a solid dark or black pill with white text, while inactive tabs are outlined or lower-contrast. Tabs are aligned from left to right and centered vertically in a strip about 40–48px tall. There is 16–24px of vertical space between the tab-switcher and the filter-bar directly below, preserving the clean separation of controls.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static control row that scrolls off-screen as the user scrolls down; it remains within the right column layout and does not stick to the viewport.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The filter-bar is positioned directly under the tab-switcher within the right column, separated by roughly 16–24px of vertical space. It spans the full width of the right column and presents as a horizontal strip of controls (dropdowns for year and client type, a compact search field, and possibly tag-style filter pills). The bar itself can be visually delineated by a subtle light gray bottom border rather than a full card, maintaining minimalism. Each control has 8–12px horizontal space between it and its neighbor, with 8–12px vertical padding within the bar. The bar’s overall height is around 56–72px, depending on the number of controls. A larger 24–32px gap separates the filter-bar from the top of the testimonials-section beneath it to indicate transition from controls to results.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard horizontal control strip that scrolls normally with its column; for longer pages it remains part of the scrollable content and does not fix to the top.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The testimonials-section occupies the main upper portion of the right column, directly below the filter-bar with about 24–32px of vertical space. It spans the entire width of the right column and is visually grouped as a single content block: a white area possibly framed by a subtle 1px light gray border and a slightly rounded corner radius that echoes the pill-style audio players from the mockup. Inside, there is 16–24px padding on all sides. The testimonial-list fills most of this section, stretching vertically, while the pagination-control sits at the bottom. The section’s height naturally expands as testimonials are loaded; above the fold, it typically occupies around half to two-thirds of the visible content area below the filters, with excess content scrollable as part of the page. A larger gap of about 40–48px separates the bottom of this section from the favorites-section beneath it.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, vertically expanding container within the right column; scrolls with the page and defines the main reading area for testimonials.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Within the testimonials-section, the testimonial-list is a vertical stack of testimonial-item cards. It begins immediately below any optional section label (if present) or near the top padding of the section, with about 8–16px of spacing from the top interior edge. The list spans the full width of the inner section minus padding, and each testimonial-item sits on its own line, occupying 100% of this inner width. There is consistent vertical spacing of approximately 16–24px between items, echoing the evenly spaced audio players in the mockup. The list is scrollable as part of the page; no internal scrollbars are used. As content grows, the list simply pushes the pagination-control further down within the same section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard vertical list inside its parent section; it grows as testimonials are added and scrolls with the page, with no fixed or sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each testimonial-item is a pill-like or rounded rectangular card that fills the width of the testimonial-list’s content area. It is approximately 72–120px tall depending on text length, with 16–24px internal padding on all sides. Visually, it follows the mockup’s pill-style audio players: a white card with a soft shadow or subtle border, rounded corners (8–16px radius), and clear separation from the background. Key information (client name, project type, year) is aligned to the left, while secondary actions (expand icon, favorite icon) are aligned to the right within the same horizontal row at the top of the card. Expanded testimonial text, when shown, appears below this header line, still inside the same card, increasing its height. There is 16–24px vertical margin between the bottom of one testimonial-item and the top of the next, creating a clear vertical scanning rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Interactive, clickable list item that maintains its position in the list; it expands or collapses vertically in place when interacted with but does not move or float elsewhere.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The favorites-section is located in the right column directly below the testimonials-section, separated by a generous 40–48px vertical gap. It spans the full width of the right column but is visually a bit more compact than the main testimonials area, signaling its auxiliary nature. The section is framed similarly: white background, subtle border or top divider line, and 16–24px of inner padding. At the top of the section, a header row runs horizontally, containing the section title aligned to the left, the favorites-counter near the title or on the right side, and the favorites-sort-control aligned to the right. Below this header row, the favorite-clients-list occupies the remainder of the section. The section’s height depends on how many favorites exist but generally sits at about one-third to half the height of the testimonials-section when populated, ensuring it feels like a concise, curated list.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static container anchored below the main testimonial content within the right column; scrolls with the page and does not overlap or float.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Inside the favorites-section, the favorites-sort-control appears in the top header row, aligned to the far right edge of the section’s inner padding. The control is typically a compact dropdown or segmented toggle, about 32–40px tall, with 8–16px horizontal padding, matching the pill-like aesthetics of other controls.
